strategies.name: Spot-Futures Cash-and-Carry
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When the futures price of a crypto asset trades at a premium to spot that exceeds the cost of capital plus fees, buying spot and selling futures locks in a risk-free yield. This basis trade is the canonical cash-and-carry arbitrage. Edge persists because retail demand for leveraged long exposure pushes futures premiums above fair value, and capital constraints prevent full arbitrage by institutional players.
strategies.entry_logic: When annualized_basis_pct exceeds min_basis_threshold after accounting for trading fees on both legs and borrowing cost, enter long spot and short futures simultaneously. Execute spot leg first (less slippage risk), then futures. For perpetuals, only enter when average 7-day funding rate exceeds min_funding_rate.
strategies.exit_logic: For dated futures: hold to expiry for guaranteed convergence. For perpetuals: exit when funding rate drops below exit_funding_threshold or when basis inverts. Unwind both legs simultaneously. Emergency exit if exchange risk materializes (withdrawal freeze, liquidation engine issues).

min_basis_threshold.name: min_basis_threshold
min_basis_threshold.description: Min annualized basis to enter (after fees)

min_funding_rate.name: min_funding_rate
min_funding_rate.description: Min 8h funding rate for perp basis trade

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per basis trade

exit_funding_threshold.name: exit_funding_threshold
exit_funding_threshold.description: Funding rate below which to exit perp trade

known_failure_modes: Exchange insolvency or withdrawal freeze traps capital
Futures liquidation cascade forces short to close at a loss
Basis stays elevated longer than capital patience allows

strategies.name: Triangular Cross-Rate Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Across three trading pairs (A/B, B/C, A/C), exchange rate relationships should be transitive. When a triangular loop yields a positive profit after fees, the mispricing is exploitable. In crypto, fragmented liquidity across trading pairs on a single exchange creates frequent micro-dislocations. Edge persists because the opportunities are small, fast, and require monitoring all possible triangles simultaneously.
strategies.entry_logic: Continuously scan all triangular paths. When triangular_profit_after_fees > min_profit_threshold, execute all three legs atomically (or as close to atomically as possible). Start with the least liquid leg to minimize adverse selection. Abort entire trade if any leg fails to fill within max_fill_time_ms.
strategies.exit_logic: No exit logic — the trade is self-closing. All three legs execute in a single atomic burst, converting back to the starting currency with profit. If partially filled, unwind the filled legs at market to minimize residual risk.

min_profit_threshold.name: min_profit_threshold
min_profit_threshold.description: Min profit per loop after all fees (as fraction)

max_fill_time_ms.name: max_fill_time_ms
max_fill_time_ms.description: Max ms to complete all three legs

min_leg_depth.name: min_leg_depth
min_leg_depth.description: Min USD-equivalent depth at each leg's price

known_failure_modes: Partial fill on one leg creates directional exposure
Fee tier changes invalidate profit calculations
Latency disadvantage against faster participants

strategies.name: CEX-DEX Price Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Centralized exchanges (CEX) and decentralized exchanges (DEX) price the same asset independently. CEX prices react faster to macro/flow events while DEX AMM pools adjust via slower arbitrageur activity. The lag creates persistent price differences of 10-100bps, especially during volatility spikes. By buying on the cheaper venue and selling on the more expensive one, the strategy captures the dislocation. Edge comes from bridging the information gap between venue types.
strategies.entry_logic: When cross_venue_spread > min_spread_threshold after accounting for CEX fees, DEX swap fees, gas costs, and slippage, execute the arbitrage. Buy on the cheaper venue and sell on the more expensive. For DEX, compute effective price including price impact for the trade size. Prioritize the DEX leg execution (higher latency) by submitting it first.
strategies.exit_logic: Self-closing — both legs execute to complete the round trip. If the DEX transaction fails or reverts, the CEX leg becomes a directional position and should be closed immediately at market. Bridge delays between chains may create temporary directional exposure.

min_spread_threshold.name: min_spread_threshold
min_spread_threshold.description: Min spread after all costs to trigger arb

max_gas_cost_pct.name: max_gas_cost_pct
max_gas_cost_pct.description: Max gas cost as pct of trade value

max_dex_slippage.name: max_dex_slippage
max_dex_slippage.description: Max allowed slippage on DEX leg

known_failure_modes: DEX transaction reverts due to slippage or MEV sandwich attack
Bridge delay creates extended directional exposure
Gas price spike makes the arb unprofitable mid-execution

strategies.name: Cross-Exchange Funding Rate Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Perpetual swap funding rates diverge across exchanges because each exchange has its own mix of long/short demand. When exchange A pays high positive funding (longs pay shorts) and exchange B pays low or negative funding, going short on A and long on B captures the funding differential with hedged directional risk. Edge persists because capital fragmentation across exchanges prevents instant equalization, and retail participants rarely monitor cross-exchange funding spreads.
strategies.entry_logic: When funding_rate_differential (A minus B) exceeds min_funding_spread for at least min_persistence_periods consecutive 8h intervals, go short on exchange A and long on exchange B. Size to equalize notional exposure. Prefer exchanges with lower counterparty risk and faster withdrawals as the long leg.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when funding differential narrows below exit_spread_threshold or when one exchange's funding rate changes sign. Also exit if unrealized PnL on either leg exceeds max_drawdown_per_leg (adverse mark-to-market, even though hedged, due to fee timing).

min_funding_spread.name: min_funding_spread
min_funding_spread.description: Min funding rate differential to enter

min_persistence_periods.name: min_persistence_periods
min_persistence_periods.description: Min consecutive 8h periods of elevated spread

max_drawdown_per_leg.name: max_drawdown_per_leg
max_drawdown_per_leg.description: Max unrealized loss per leg before exit

known_failure_modes: Exchange-specific liquidation cascade causes one leg to be force-closed
Withdrawal delays prevent capital rebalancing between exchanges
Funding rate calculation methodology changes on one exchange

strategies.name: ETF Premium/Discount to NAV
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Crypto and commodity ETFs (e.g., BTC ETFs, gold ETFs) periodically trade at premiums or discounts to their net asset value. When the premium/discount exceeds the creation/redemption cost plus fees, the convergence is economically guaranteed by authorized participants. For non-redeemable structures (like closed-end funds), the premium/ discount can persist but mean-reverts on sentiment shifts. Edge is the structural convergence mechanism for open-end ETFs and behavioral mean-reversion for closed-end.
strategies.entry_logic: For open-end ETFs: when |premium_discount| > creation_redemption_cost + entry_buffer, trade the convergence. Buy at discount, sell at premium versus hedging with the underlying. For closed-end: trade when premium_zscore exceeds zscore_entry_threshold, fading the extreme.
strategies.exit_logic: For open-end: exit when premium/discount narrows to within exit_threshold of fair value. For closed-end: exit when z-score returns to within zscore_exit_band. Stop loss at max_divergence (rare for open-end, common for closed-end).

entry_buffer.name: entry_buffer
entry_buffer.description: Buffer above creation/redemption cost for entry

zscore_entry_threshold.name: zscore_entry_threshold
zscore_entry_threshold.description: Z-score threshold for closed-end fund entry

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per ETF trade

known_failure_modes: Authorized participant mechanism breaks down (market stress)
Closed-end fund discount widens to historic levels without reverting
Underlying asset liquidity dries up, making NAV calculation unreliable

strategies.name: Stablecoin De-Peg Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Stablecoins pegged to $1.00 periodically trade at premiums or discounts due to redemption queue congestion, fear events, or demand surges. When a high-quality stablecoin (backed by reserves, audited) deviates from peg by more than the round-trip redemption cost, buying the discounted stablecoin and redeeming at par captures the spread. Edge is capital availability and willingness to take counterparty risk during fear-driven de-pegs when most participants are fleeing.
strategies.entry_logic: When peg_deviation > min_depeg_threshold AND reserve_health_score > min_reserve_health (the stablecoin is fundamentally sound), buy the discounted stablecoin. Scale size with deviation magnitude and reserve quality. Avoid buying if redemption_queue exceeds max_queue_threshold (redemption mechanism may be impaired).
strategies.exit_logic: Redeem at par through the issuer, or sell on market when price recovers to within exit_peg_band of $1.00. Time limit of max_hold_days for market-based exit. Immediate exit if reserve_health drops below emergency_health_threshold.

min_depeg_threshold.name: min_depeg_threshold
min_depeg_threshold.description: Min peg deviation to trigger entry

min_reserve_health.name: min_reserve_health
min_reserve_health.description: Min reserve health score to enter

exit_peg_band.name: exit_peg_band
exit_peg_band.description: Price band around $1.00 to trigger exit

max_hold_days.name: max_hold_days
max_hold_days.description: Max days to hold de-peg position

known_failure_modes: Stablecoin is fundamentally insolvent — reserves are fraudulent
Redemption mechanism is permanently impaired
De-peg cascades to other stablecoins, creating systemic risk

strategies.name: Prediction-Market-to-Equity Cross-Asset Arb
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market contracts on company events (FDA approval, antitrust ruling, merger completion) imply probabilities that can be translated to expected stock price outcomes. When the prediction market implied probability diverges from the equity options-implied probability, one market is mispriced. By buying the cheaper probability expression and selling the richer one, the strategy captures the convergence. Edge is cross-market inefficiency — prediction market participants and options market participants rarely monitor each other.
strategies.entry_logic: Map the prediction market contract to an options-implied probability (using binary barrier pricing or digital replication). When |pred_prob - options_prob| > min_prob_spread, buy the cheaper probability expression and sell the richer. For prediction market cheap: buy prediction contract + buy protective equity options. For options cheap: sell prediction contract + buy equity options structure.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold both legs through event resolution. Early exit if spread narrows to within convergence_threshold (take profit on convergence). Stop loss if spread widens beyond max_spread (relationship breakdown).

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per cross-asset arb

known_failure_modes: Binary barrier model mis-maps the prediction market outcome to equity price
Options liquidity is insufficient for the hedge structure
Prediction market and options resolve under different conditions

strategies.name: Dual-Listed Equity Spread
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Companies dual-listed on multiple exchanges (e.g., US ADR vs home market) should trade at parity adjusted for FX and withholding taxes. During market stress or when one market is closed, temporary dislocations of 20-100bps emerge. By buying the cheap listing and selling the expensive one, the strategy captures the convergence that is guaranteed by the fungibility of the underlying shares. Edge is speed of execution across time zones and capital availability during stress.
strategies.entry_logic: When parity_spread_zscore exceeds entry_zscore, buy the cheap listing and sell the expensive one. Hedge FX exposure unless the spread is large enough to absorb FX risk. Prefer entry during overlapping market hours when both listings are actively trading.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when spread normalizes (z-score within exit_band). Also exit if FX moves adversely beyond fx_stop_threshold. Time stop at max_hold_days if spread persists (structural reason may have emerged).

fx_stop_threshold.name: fx_stop_threshold
fx_stop_threshold.description: FX adverse move to trigger stop

known_failure_modes: Listing fungibility is impaired (capital controls, regulatory change)
FX move overwhelms the spread profit
One listing is subject to different settlement or tax rules

strategies.name: Synthetic Replication Mispricing
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: A synthetic position (e.g., long call + short put at the same strike = synthetic long, or a combination of prediction market contracts that replicate another) should trade at parity with the actual instrument. When the synthetic is cheaper than the actual or vice versa, the mispricing is exploitable. In prediction markets, complex combinations of contracts can replicate simple outcomes. Edge comes from the computational overhead of identifying and monitoring all valid synthetic relationships.
strategies.entry_logic: When parity_spread > min_parity_spread after accounting for total fees across all legs, buy the cheap side and sell the rich side. Limit synthetics to max_legs (fewer legs = less execution risk). Execute the least liquid leg first. Abort if any leg slips beyond per_leg_max_slip.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to convergence (expiry/resolution). Early exit if spread normalizes. If partially filled, unwind to flatten exposure. No directional stop — trade is market-neutral by construction.

min_parity_spread.name: min_parity_spread
min_parity_spread.description: Min spread between actual and synthetic

per_leg_max_slip.name: per_leg_max_slip
per_leg_max_slip.description: Max slippage per leg before aborting

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per synthetic arb

known_failure_modes: Synthetic equivalence assumptions break under edge cases
Leg execution risk compounds with more legs
Parity only holds at expiry — mark-to-market can diverge

strategies.name: Index Rebalance Front-Run
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When indices (S&P 500, Russell, crypto indices) announce rebalancing — adding or removing constituents — the affected stocks experience predictable buying or selling pressure from passive funds. Added stocks are bought and removed stocks are sold, creating a temporary supply/demand imbalance. Prediction market contracts on index inclusion also re-price. By positioning before the passive flow executes, the strategy captures the mechanical demand/supply shift. Edge is structural — passive funds MUST rebalance, and the timing is known in advance.
strategies.entry_logic: After rebalance announcement, buy stocks being added (or buy prediction market YES contracts on inclusion). Short stocks being removed. Enter within entry_window_days of announcement. Size proportional to estimated_passive_flow relative to stock ADV. Cap at max_position_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit on the effective rebalance date or within exit_window_days after. The passive flow should be complete, and further holding has no structural edge. Stop loss at stop_loss_pct if the trade goes against before rebalance (market anticipation was wrong).

exit_window_days.name: exit_window_days
exit_window_days.description: Days after effective date to hold

stop_loss_pct.name: stop_loss_pct
stop_loss_pct.description: Stop loss pct on pre-rebalance adverse move

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per rebalance trade

known_failure_modes: Rebalance announcement is widely anticipated — no edge left
Index methodology changes or rebalance is delayed
Market conditions cause passive funds to deviate from mechanical rebalancing

strategies.name: Futures Roll Yield Capture
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Futures curves in contango (front month cheaper than back months) provide positive roll yield to short positions, while backwardated curves provide positive roll yield to long positions. By systematically positioning to capture roll yield — long when in backwardation, short when in contango — the strategy harvests the term structure premium. Edge is the structural tendency for commodity and financial futures to spend more time in contango (storage costs, insurance premiums) than backwardation, creating a persistent carry source.
strategies.entry_logic: When annualized_roll_yield exceeds min_carry AND curve_shape_zscore indicates the carry is richer than usual (above carry_zscore_threshold), enter the carry position. Long in backwardation, short in contango. Roll positions roll_days_before expiry to avoid delivery risk. Size based on carry-to-risk ratio.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when roll yield flips sign or drops below exit_carry_threshold. Roll to next contract pair before expiry. Stop loss at stop_loss_pct on mark-to-market (the carry may not compensate for spot price movement against the position).

min_carry.name: min_carry
min_carry.description: Min annualized roll yield to enter

known_failure_modes: Spot price moves sharply against the carry direction
Curve shape changes rapidly (contango to backwardation)
Roll liquidity is thin, causing unfavorable roll execution

strategies.name: FX Interest Rate Differential Carry
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: High-yielding currencies earn interest rate differential carry versus low-yielding currencies. The uncovered interest rate parity theorem predicts that FX moves should offset the carry, but empirically they do not — the "forward premium puzzle" means high-yielders tend to appreciate rather than depreciate. By going long high-yield currencies and short low-yield currencies, the strategy captures both the interest differential and the FX appreciation bias. Edge is the well-documented forward premium anomaly.
strategies.entry_logic: Rank G10 currencies by interest rate differential. Go long top 3 (highest carry), short bottom 3 (lowest carry). Weight by carry-to-vol ratio (higher ratio = larger weight). Rebalance at rebalance_frequency intervals. Apply vol filter: reduce positions when VIX > max_vix_for_carry (carry trades crash during risk-off).
strategies.exit_logic: Rebalance-based exits. Reduce all positions when VIX exceeds max_vix_for_carry. Full exit if carry-to-vol ratio drops below min_carry_vol_ratio across the basket. Individual currency exits when interest rate differential flips.

min_rate_differential.name: min_rate_differential
min_rate_differential.description: Min interest rate diff to include a currency

max_vix_for_carry.name: max_vix_for_carry
max_vix_for_carry.description: VIX above which to reduce carry positions

known_failure_modes: Risk-off event causes simultaneous crash of all high-yielders
Central bank surprise changes rate differential abruptly
Carry-to-vol ratio is positive but absolute carry is too small for costs

strategies.name: Crypto Lending Rate Carry
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: DeFi lending protocols offer variable interest rates that fluctuate with demand for leverage. During high-demand periods, stablecoin lending rates spike to 10-50% APY. By deploying capital to lending protocols when rates are elevated and withdrawing when rates normalize, the strategy captures above-market yields. The carry exceeds risk-free rates because of smart contract risk, liquidity risk, and counterparty concentration risk that most capital is unwilling to bear.
strategies.entry_logic: When current_lending_apy exceeds min_apy AND the protocol passes risk checks (tvl > min_tvl, smart_contract_risk_score < max_risk, utilization_rate < max_utilization), deploy stablecoins to the lending pool. Diversify across max_protocol_count protocols. Cap exposure per protocol at max_per_protocol_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Withdraw when lending_apy drops below exit_apy or when risk score increases above max_risk. Also withdraw if utilization_rate exceeds emergency_utilization (liquidity at risk). Immediate withdrawal on any smart contract incident.

min_apy.name: min_apy
min_apy.description: Min lending APY to deploy capital

known_failure_modes: Smart contract exploit drains protocol funds
High utilization prevents withdrawal when needed
Stablecoin de-peg creates loss on the lending asset itself

strategies.name: Dividend Capture with Hedge
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Stocks drop by approximately the dividend amount on ex-dividend date, but the drop is often less than the full dividend due to tax considerations and institutional buying pressure around ex-dates. By buying the stock before ex-date, capturing the dividend, and hedging with a short futures or options position to protect against price decline, the strategy captures the portion of the dividend that is not offset by price drop. Edge is the tax-driven inefficiency in dividend pricing.
strategies.entry_logic: Buy the stock entry_days_before ex-date. Simultaneously sell a short-dated call (covered call) or buy a put for downside protection. Only enter when the hedge cost is less than expected underdrop (dividend minus expected price decline). Prefer high-yield stocks where the underdrop historically averages > min_underdrop_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit on or the day after ex-date. The dividend is captured, the hedge is closed. If the stock drops more than expected, the hedge limits the loss. If the stock drops less, the full underdrop is profit.

min_dividend_yield.name: min_dividend_yield
min_dividend_yield.description: Min quarterly dividend yield to consider

min_underdrop_pct.name: min_underdrop_pct
min_underdrop_pct.description: Min expected underdrop as pct of dividend

max_hedge_cost_pct.name: max_hedge_cost_pct
max_hedge_cost_pct.description: Max hedge cost as pct of dividend

known_failure_modes: Stock drops more than the dividend (bad news coincides with ex-date)
Hedge cost exceeds the underdrop profit
Dividend is cut or cancelled after position entry

strategies.name: Prediction Market Time Value Decay
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market contracts that are very likely to resolve YES (price > 0.90) or very likely NO (price < 0.10) have embedded time value — the probability of an unlikely reversal commands a premium that decays as resolution approaches. By buying contracts priced > 0.90 and holding to resolution, the strategy earns the 0-10 cent time value decay as the certain outcome approaches. This is analogous to selling deep OTM options theta — the time premium decays as the event nears. Edge is the willingness to accept capital lockup risk for a high-probability payout.
strategies.entry_logic: Buy contracts priced above min_confidence_price when the annualized yield (1.00 - price) / days_to_resolution * 365 exceeds min_annualized_yield. Require model_resolution_probability > model_min_prob to confirm the near-certain outcome. Diversify across max_contracts to reduce single-event risk. Avoid contracts where reversal rate at current price historically exceeds max_reversal_rate.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to resolution for full payout. Early exit only if model_resolution_probability drops below emergency_exit_prob (fundamentals have shifted). No trail stop — binary payout.

min_confidence_price.name: min_confidence_price
min_confidence_price.description: Min contract price (confidence level) to buy

min_annualized_yield.name: min_annualized_yield
min_annualized_yield.description: Min annualized yield from time value capture

max_contracts.name: max_contracts
max_contracts.description: Max number of contracts to diversify across

known_failure_modes: Black swan reversal — the 'certain' outcome doesn't happen
Resolution is delayed, extending capital lockup and reducing annualized yield
Model probability is wrong — contract is not as certain as it appears

strategies.name: Commodity Seasonality Pattern
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Commodities exhibit strong seasonal price patterns driven by production cycles (harvest, planting), consumption cycles (heating oil in winter, gasoline in summer), and storage costs. These seasonal tendencies persist because the underlying physical drivers are structural and recurring. By systematically entering seasonal trades when the current year's price action aligns with historical seasonal patterns, the strategy captures predictable supply/demand shifts. Edge is the physical basis of the seasonal pattern, which financial speculation alone cannot eliminate.
strategies.entry_logic: Enter seasonal trades when seasonal_win_rate for the upcoming period exceeds min_win_rate AND current inventory levels confirm the seasonal thesis (e.g., low heating oil inventory before winter strengthens the long signal). Enter entry_days_before the historical seasonal window begins. Avoid if weather_forecast_anomaly contradicts the seasonal thesis.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit at the end of the seasonal window, or when inventory levels normalize (seasonal driver has played out). Stop loss at stop_pct. Early exit if weather or supply shock invalidates the seasonal thesis.

min_win_rate.name: min_win_rate
min_win_rate.description: Min historical win rate for seasonal period

entry_days_before.name: entry_days_before
entry_days_before.description: Days before seasonal window to enter

stop_pct.name: stop_pct
stop_pct.description: Stop loss as pct of position

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per seasonal trade

known_failure_modes: Structural shift in supply (new production region) breaks seasonal pattern
Weather anomaly overwhelms the seasonal tendency
Geopolitical disruption (export ban, sanctions) overrides normal flow

strategies.name: Inventory Report Surprise Trade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Weekly commodity inventory reports (EIA crude, USDA grains, LME metals) cause immediate price reactions when actuals differ from consensus. The initial price reaction captures only 70-80% of the total adjustment, with the remaining drift occurring over the next 1-4 hours as the market processes implications. By quantifying the surprise (actual vs consensus) and trading the continuation, the strategy captures the post-report drift. Edge is speed of interpretation and systematic surprise quantification.
strategies.entry_logic: On inventory report release, compute surprise_zscore. If |zscore| > entry_surprise_threshold, enter in the direction of the fundamental implication (draw = bullish, build = bearish). Wait reaction_delay_seconds after release to enter (let initial spike pass). Scale size by surprise magnitude. Enter with limit orders at the post-spike price.
strategies.exit_logic: Target the model-implied full price adjustment. Trail stop at trail_pct. Time stop at max_hold_hours. Exit immediately if report is revised or corrected.

reaction_delay_seconds.name: reaction_delay_seconds
reaction_delay_seconds.description: Seconds to wait after report release

known_failure_modes: Consensus estimate was stale — surprise is already partially priced
Report methodology change makes historical comparisons invalid
Simultaneous conflicting reports create ambiguous signal

strategies.name: Refined Product Crack Spread
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The crack spread (difference between refined product prices and crude oil) reflects refinery margins. When crack spreads are historically extreme, they mean-revert because refiners adjust output (high margins = increase production, which increases crude demand and refined product supply, compressing margins). By fading extreme crack spreads, the strategy captures the mean-reversion driven by the physical refining supply response. Edge is the structural production response that guarantees mean-reversion over medium-term horizons.
strategies.entry_logic: When crack_spread_zscore exceeds entry_zscore (margins too high): sell gasoline, buy crude (short crack). When below -entry_zscore (margins too compressed): buy gasoline, sell crude (long crack). Confirm with refinery utilization — high utilization + high margins = production increase coming = short crack signal strengthened.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when crack spread z-score normalizes (within exit_zscore_band). Stop loss at stop_zscore. Time stop at max_hold_weeks to avoid extended drawdowns in structural shift scenarios.

entry_zscore.name: entry_zscore
entry_zscore.description: Z-score threshold for crack spread entry

known_failure_modes: Refinery outage prevents the supply response (hurricane, maintenance)
Regulatory change (emissions standards) structurally shifts crack spread
Crude supply shock overwhelms the crack spread relationship

strategies.name: Perpetual Funding Rate Harvest
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Perpetual swap funding rates are systematically positive (longs pay shorts) during bull markets because retail demand for leveraged long exposure exceeds supply. By holding a delta-neutral position (long spot, short perp), the strategy collects positive funding without directional risk. Edge is the structural demand imbalance created by retail leverage appetite and the willingness to accept capital lock-up risk on exchanges.
strategies.entry_logic: When 7d_average_funding_rate > min_avg_funding AND annualized yield exceeds min_annualized_yield, deploy capital: buy spot, short perp at equal notional. Spread capital across max 3 exchanges to reduce counterparty risk. Enter when funding is about to be charged (within 1 hour of funding timestamp) for immediate collection.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when 7d_average_funding_rate drops below exit_funding_threshold (yield no longer compensates for exchange risk). Also exit if exchange risk indicators trigger (unusual withdrawal delays, regulatory news). Unwind both legs simultaneously.

min_avg_funding.name: min_avg_funding
min_avg_funding.description: Min 7d avg funding rate (per 8h) to enter

exit_funding_threshold.name: exit_funding_threshold
exit_funding_threshold.description: Funding rate below which to exit

max_exchange_concentration.name: max_exchange_concentration
max_exchange_concentration.description: Max portfolio pct on any single exchange

known_failure_modes: Exchange insolvency or withdrawal freeze
Funding rate turns persistently negative in bear market
Short perp gets liquidated during rapid price spike

strategies.name: DEX Backrun Opportunity
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Large DEX swaps create temporary price dislocations between the AMM pool and the broader market. By detecting pending large swaps in the mempool and placing a transaction immediately after (backrunning), the strategy captures the price correction as the AMM pool price reverts toward the true market price. Unlike frontrunning, backrunning is non-predatory — it corrects a dislocation rather than exploiting the original trader. Edge is speed and mempool monitoring infrastructure.
strategies.entry_logic: Monitor mempool for large swap transactions. When a pending swap's expected_price_impact > min_impact_threshold, calculate the backrun profit (buy/sell the dislocation after the swap executes). Submit the backrun transaction with gas price sufficient to be included in the same block but after the target swap. Only execute if backrun_profit > min_profit_after_gas.
strategies.exit_logic: Atomic execution — the backrun transaction completes the round trip in a single block. No holding period. If the transaction reverts, only gas is lost.

min_impact_threshold.name: min_impact_threshold
min_impact_threshold.description: Min price impact of target swap to trigger backrun

min_profit_after_gas.name: min_profit_after_gas
min_profit_after_gas.description: Min profit in USD after gas costs

max_gas_price_gwei.name: max_gas_price_gwei
max_gas_price_gwei.description: Max gas price willing to pay in gwei

known_failure_modes: Competing backrunners drive up gas costs, eliminating profit
Target transaction reverts, wasting gas
Smart contract vulnerability in backrun bundle

strategies.name: On-Chain Flow Analysis
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Large on-chain transfers to/from exchanges are leading indicators of price moves. Inflows to exchanges precede selling pressure (users deposit to sell). Outflows from exchanges precede accumulation phases (users withdraw to hold). By tracking whale wallet movements and exchange netflow in real-time, the strategy positions ahead of the flow's impact on order books. Edge is monitoring the blockchain directly rather than waiting for exchange-level data.
strategies.entry_logic: When exchange_netflow is significantly positive (net inflow, z-score > inflow_threshold), go short (selling pressure incoming). When significantly negative (net outflow, z-score < -inflow_threshold), go long (accumulation). Weight by whale_transfer_volume (larger transfers = stronger signal). Require confirmation from exchange_reserve_change_24h.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when netflow normalizes (z-score returns to within neutral_band). Trail stop at trail_pct. Time stop at max_hold_hours.
known_failure_modes: Exchange hot wallet movements mistaken for user flows
Inflow is for staking/DeFi, not selling
Whale wallet labeling is inaccurate

strategies.name: Liquidation Cascade Fade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Crypto liquidation cascades (forced closing of leveraged positions) create temporary price overshoots beyond fundamental value. By identifying when a cascade has exhausted (liquidation volume drops sharply after a spike, open interest has contracted significantly), the strategy buys the overshoot, expecting a bounce as the forced selling ends. Edge is patience — entering after the cascade rather than trying to catch the falling knife during it.
strategies.entry_logic: After liquidation_volume_zscore spikes above cascade_threshold and then drops back below cascade_end_threshold (cascade exhausting), AND open_interest has contracted by at least oi_contraction_pct, enter long. Require funding rate to have turned negative (shorts dominant = contrarian buy signal). Enter with limit orders below current price as price may still be volatile.
strategies.exit_logic: Target recovery of bounce_target_pct of the cascade move. Trail stop at trail_pct. Stop loss at stop_below_low if new low is made (cascade not actually over). Time stop at max_hold_hours.

cascade_threshold.name: cascade_threshold
cascade_threshold.description: Z-score threshold to identify cascade start

cascade_end_threshold.name: cascade_end_threshold
cascade_end_threshold.description: Z-score threshold to identify cascade exhaustion

oi_contraction_pct.name: oi_contraction_pct
oi_contraction_pct.description: Min OI contraction to confirm liquidation wash

bounce_target_pct.name: bounce_target_pct
bounce_target_pct.description: Target bounce as pct of cascade move

known_failure_modes: Cascade is not over — second wave of liquidations follows
Fundamental news justifies the price drop — no bounce
Exchange issues prevent timely execution during volatile period

strategies.name: Token Airdrop Event Strategy
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Token airdrops create predictable price patterns: anticipation buying before the snapshot, sell pressure from recipients after distribution, and eventual price stabilization. By mapping the airdrop lifecycle (announcement, snapshot, distribution, vesting) and trading each phase appropriately, the strategy captures the predictable behavioral dynamics. Edge is systematic tracking and timing of airdrop events that most participants engage with ad-hoc.
strategies.entry_logic: Pre-snapshot: buy the parent token within pre_snapshot_days before the snapshot date (anticipation buying). Post-distribution: short the airdropped token starting at distribution + wait_days (sell pressure from recipients) only if sell_pressure_estimate is high. Size based on estimated airdrop value relative to parent token market cap.
strategies.exit_logic: Pre-snapshot position: exit at or shortly after the snapshot date (sell the news). Post-distribution short: cover when sell pressure subsides (daily selling volume declines below baseline) or at max_hold_days.

wait_days_post_distribution.name: wait_days_post_distribution
wait_days_post_distribution.description: Days after distribution to begin shorting

max_hold_days.name: max_hold_days
max_hold_days.description: Max hold days for post-distribution short

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per airdrop trade

known_failure_modes: Airdrop details change (snapshot date moved, eligibility criteria altered)
Massive buy-the-rumor sell-the-news causes pre-snapshot position to lose
Airdropped token has strong demand that overwhelms sell pressure

strategies.name: Bitcoin Hashrate Divergence
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Bitcoin's hashrate reflects miners' economic conviction — miners expand capacity when they expect higher future BTC prices and contract when they expect lower prices. When hashrate is trending up while BTC price is flat or declining, it signals miner conviction that prices will recover (they are investing in infrastructure despite current low profitability). Conversely, declining hashrate during a price rally signals miners are unconvinced and may be selling. Edge is extracting a fundamental demand signal from the physical mining economy that most traders ignore.
strategies.entry_logic: When hashrate_price_divergence_score exceeds divergence_threshold (hashrate rising while price falling = bullish divergence), go long BTC. When hashrate declining while price rising = bearish divergence, go short or reduce long exposure. Confirm with mining_difficulty adjustment direction. Enter gradually over entry_window_days.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when divergence score normalizes (hashrate and price move in same direction). Trail stop at trail_pct. Time stop at max_hold_weeks. Exit if a halving event is imminent (changes the economics fundamentally).

divergence_threshold.name: divergence_threshold
divergence_threshold.description: Min divergence score to trigger entry

entry_window_days.name: entry_window_days
entry_window_days.description: Days over which to build position

known_failure_modes: Hashrate increase is from a single large miner, not broad conviction
China mining ban or similar regulatory shock distorts hashrate signal
New ASIC release causes hashrate jump unrelated to price expectations

strategies.name: Economic Data Release Momentum
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Scheduled economic releases (CPI, NFP, GDP, PMI) cause prediction market prices to adjust rapidly, but the adjustment often undershoots in the first 5-30 minutes because participants disagree on the magnitude of the surprise. By comparing the actual release to consensus and historical surprise-to-price mappings, the strategy trades the continuation of the initial move before the market fully prices the new information. Edge is speed of interpretation.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute surprise = actual - consensus, normalized by historical surprise stdev. If surprise_zscore > entry_surprise_threshold, enter in the direction implied by historical price-impact mapping. Enter only after reaction_delay_seconds to let the initial spike pass and confirm direction. Scale size by surprise magnitude but cap at max_position_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Target exit at model-implied full adjustment level. Trail stop at trail_pct behind the current price. Hard time stop at max_hold_minutes after entry. Exit immediately if a contradicting release occurs or if price reverses past the pre-release level.

entry_surprise_threshold.name: entry_surprise_threshold
entry_surprise_threshold.description: Minimum surprise z-score to trigger entry

reaction_delay_seconds.name: reaction_delay_seconds
reaction_delay_seconds.description: Seconds to wait after release before entering

trail_pct.name: trail_pct
trail_pct.description: Trailing stop as pct of price

known_failure_modes: Release is revised or corrected shortly after publication
Historical price-impact mapping has changed due to regime shift
Simultaneous releases create conflicting signals

strategies.name: FOMC Decision Pre/Post Drift
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The FOMC meeting cycle creates a well-documented pre-announcement drift (markets tend to drift upward in the 24 hours before FOMC) and a post-decision adjustment pattern. In prediction markets, contracts tied to rate decisions exhibit systematic mispricing 12-24 hours pre-meeting because retail participants either over-hedge or under-position. The strategy captures the convergence toward fair value as the meeting approaches and the post-decision momentum as the market digests the statement language.
strategies.entry_logic: Pre-FOMC: If the prediction market rate contract price deviates from fed funds futures implied probability by more than pre_drift_threshold, trade toward the futures-implied price starting at entry_hours_before the decision. Post-FOMC: After the decision, if the statement language delta score (hawkish/dovish shift) exceeds post_language_threshold, trade the continuation for the post-decision drift.
strategies.exit_logic: Pre-FOMC positions exit at the decision announcement. Post-FOMC positions target the historical post-drift completion (typically 2-4 hours) or exit at max_post_hold_hours. Stop loss at stop_loss_pct for both phases.

pre_drift_threshold.name: pre_drift_threshold
pre_drift_threshold.description: Min deviation from futures-implied price for pre-drift entry

entry_hours_before.name: entry_hours_before
entry_hours_before.description: Hours before FOMC to enter pre-drift position

post_language_threshold.name: post_language_threshold
post_language_threshold.description: Min language delta score for post-decision entry

known_failure_modes: FOMC surprises with emergency action outside normal schedule
Statement language parsing fails on novel phrasing
Pre-drift is front-run by fast HFT participants

strategies.name: Election Poll Aggregation Alpha
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market election contracts are heavily influenced by the latest headline poll, but individual polls are noisy. A proper Bayesian poll aggregation model (weighting by sample size, methodology quality, recency, and house effects) produces more accurate probability estimates than the market. When the aggregation model's fair value diverges from the market price, the model is systematically closer to the eventual outcome. Edge comes from superior signal processing versus the median market participant.
strategies.entry_logic: When |model_probability - market_price| > entry_divergence_threshold, trade in the direction of the model. Scale size by divergence magnitude and model confidence (inverse of posterior variance). Increase position as election approaches if divergence persists (information should converge). Reduce threshold for entry as election nears (less time for convergence, but also less noise).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when divergence narrows to within convergence_threshold, or add to position on new polls that reinforce the model view. Time-stop at max_hold_days or election day, whichever comes first. Stop loss if model probability itself flips direction.

model_confidence_min.name: model_confidence_min
model_confidence_min.description: Minimum model confidence to act on signal

known_failure_modes: Systematic polling error — all polls are biased in the same direction
October surprise invalidates all prior poll data
Market is pricing information the model doesn't capture (ground game, turnout models)

strategies.name: Supreme Court Oral Argument Signal
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Research shows that the direction and intensity of Supreme Court justice questions during oral arguments are predictive of case outcomes. Prediction markets for court rulings often do not fully incorporate this signal because most participants lack legal expertise. By parsing oral argument transcripts for question hostility scores and mapping to historical ruling patterns, the strategy positions ahead of the ruling. Edge is informational asymmetry between legal analysis and market participants.
strategies.entry_logic: After oral arguments are published, compute justice-level hostility scores and map to predicted outcome probabilities using historical patterns. If model_implied_probability diverges from market_price by more than entry_threshold, enter in the model's direction. Weight by number of historical analogues (more data = higher confidence). Enter within entry_window_hours of transcript publication for maximum edge.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to ruling announcement. If new information (amicus briefs, related rulings) shifts model probability, adjust position accordingly. Stop loss if model flips direction by more than flip_threshold.

entry_threshold.name: entry_threshold
entry_threshold.description: Min model-vs-market gap after oral arguments

entry_window_hours.name: entry_window_hours
entry_window_hours.description: Hours after transcript release to enter

min_historical_analogues.name: min_historical_analogues
min_historical_analogues.description: Minimum historical cases for model confidence

flip_threshold.name: flip_threshold
flip_threshold.description: Model probability shift to trigger stop

known_failure_modes: Justices use questioning style strategically, not reflecting their vote intention
Narrow ruling or unexpected procedural outcome voids the binary contract
Long delay between arguments and ruling creates opportunity cost

strategies.name: Earnings Surprise Straddle Proxy
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When prediction market contracts exist for earnings-related outcomes (revenue beats, EPS ranges), the implied move can be compared to equity options implied volatility. If prediction markets under-price the probability of a large surprise relative to options-implied distributions, buying both tail outcomes in the prediction market is a cheaper way to express a straddle-like payoff. Edge comes from prediction markets being less efficient at pricing tail probabilities than options markets.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute the probability of a large earnings surprise from options (implied move > 1.5 stdev). Compare to the sum of prediction market tail contract prices. If options-implied tail probability exceeds prediction_market tail probability by spread_threshold, buy both tail contracts in the prediction market. Enter within entry_window_days of earnings.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold through earnings. One tail contract goes to 1.00 or both expire worthless. Early exit only if the spread normalizes pre-earnings (take profit on the relative value convergence). No stop loss — binary payoff.

spread_threshold.name: spread_threshold
spread_threshold.description: Min probability spread between options and prediction market tails

entry_window_days.name: entry_window_days
entry_window_days.description: Days before earnings to enter position

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per straddle proxy

min_options_iv_percentile.name: min_options_iv_percentile
min_options_iv_percentile.description: Minimum IV percentile for the comparison to be meaningful

known_failure_modes: Options IV is inflated by hedging demand, not genuine surprise probability
Prediction market tail contracts are too illiquid to fill at model prices
Earnings are in line — both tails expire worthless

strategies.name: Weather Forecast Model Divergence
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Weather prediction markets (temperature ranges, hurricane landfalls, precipitation) are often priced off a single popular forecast model (e.g., GFS). When ensemble models (ECMWF, GFS, NAM) disagree, the market typically anchors to the most popular model. By weighting ensemble forecasts more accurately and detecting when the market is over-indexed on a single model, the strategy trades the convergence toward the ensemble mean. Edge is informational — most prediction market participants lack meteorological sophistication.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute ensemble-weighted forecast and compare to market-implied value. When |ensemble_value - market_implied| > entry_divergence AND forecast_spread_across_models < max_model_spread (models disagree within a bounded range but market is outside that range), enter toward the ensemble mean. Higher weight given to models with better recent accuracy for this metric.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when market converges to within convergence_cents of ensemble value, or when forecast updates bring ensemble closer to market (edge disappears). Hard exit at resolution. Stop loss if ensemble itself shifts to agree with market (model update).

entry_divergence.name: entry_divergence
entry_divergence.description: Min divergence between ensemble and market

max_model_spread.name: max_model_spread
max_model_spread.description: Max inter-model disagreement for signal validity

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per weather trade

known_failure_modes: All models are wrong in the same direction due to unusual atmospheric conditions
Market is pricing local information (observer reports) not in models
Rapid model updates cause whipsawing

strategies.name: Regulatory Filing Lead-Lag
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Public regulatory filings (SEC, FDA, FCC) contain actionable information that prediction market participants are slow to incorporate because they don't monitor filing databases in real-time. By scraping EDGAR, FDA calendars, and FCC dockets, the strategy detects relevant filings within minutes and trades prediction market contracts before the broader market reacts (typically 1-4 hours lag for non-headline filings). Edge is pure speed of information acquisition.
strategies.entry_logic: When a relevant filing is detected (relevance_score > min_relevance), assess directional impact and enter if expected_price_impact exceeds entry_impact_threshold. Enter within max_entry_delay_minutes of filing publication. Prioritize contracts where the filing is most material and least likely to have been anticipated.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price reaches target_move_pct of expected impact, or at max_hold_hours (after which information is fully diffused). Stop loss at stop_loss_pct. Exit immediately if a subsequent filing contradicts the initial signal.

min_relevance.name: min_relevance
min_relevance.description: Minimum filing relevance score to act

entry_impact_threshold.name: entry_impact_threshold
entry_impact_threshold.description: Min expected price impact to justify entry

max_entry_delay_minutes.name: max_entry_delay_minutes
max_entry_delay_minutes.description: Max minutes after filing to still enter

max_hold_hours.name: max_hold_hours
max_hold_hours.description: Max hold time for information edge

known_failure_modes: Filing relevance model misclassifies a filing as material
Market already priced the filing content from leaks or earlier signals
Filing is amended or withdrawn shortly after publication

strategies.name: Geopolitical Escalation Gradient
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Geopolitical tensions follow an escalation ladder — diplomatic warnings, sanctions, military posturing, kinetic action. Each rung on the ladder has a historical probability of escalating further. Prediction market contracts on geopolitical outcomes tend to over-react to early escalation steps (jumping to high probabilities too fast) and under-react to later steps (anchoring after desensitization). By mapping the current escalation level to historical base rates, the strategy fades overreaction and joins underreaction. Edge is calibration — most participants use availability heuristic rather than base rates.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute the model-implied probability of the contract outcome based on current escalation level and historical transition probabilities. When |model_prob - market_price| > entry_threshold, trade toward model. In early escalation (levels 1-3), this typically means fading market panic. In late escalation (levels 7+), this means buying underpriced escalation contracts.
strategies.exit_logic: Recompute model probability on each escalation level change and adjust position. Exit when divergence narrows below exit_threshold, or on de-escalation (escalation level drops by 2+). Stop loss if model probability itself shifts dramatically on a single event.

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct for geopolitical trades

min_historical_analogues.name: min_historical_analogues
min_historical_analogues.description: Minimum historical escalation sequences for base rate

known_failure_modes: This time is different — historical analogues don't apply to novel geopolitical context
Black swan escalation jump skips multiple levels instantly
Base rate model lacks granularity for the specific scenario

strategies.name: Regulatory Deadline Squeeze
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Many prediction market contracts have regulatory or legal deadlines (FDA approval date, debt ceiling deadline, trade agreement expiry). As the deadline approaches, contracts that have been trading at moderate probabilities (30-70%) tend to exhibit a squeeze toward one extreme as the resolution becomes imminent. The direction of the squeeze is predictable from procedural signals (filings, public statements, committee votes). Edge comes from tracking procedural progress more closely than the market.
strategies.entry_logic: When days_to_deadline < entry_window_days AND procedural_progress_score strongly favors one direction (> directional_threshold), enter in that direction. If procedural signals are mixed, do not trade. Increase position conviction as deadline approaches and procedural signals strengthen. Only enter when market price has not yet fully reflected procedural reality.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold through resolution or exit if procedural signals flip (progress score reverses beyond flip_threshold). Take partial profits at 50% of expected move. No time stop — the deadline IS the catalyst.

entry_window_days.name: entry_window_days
entry_window_days.description: Days before deadline to start monitoring for entry

directional_threshold.name: directional_threshold
directional_threshold.description: Min procedural progress score for directional conviction

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per deadline trade

known_failure_modes: Deadline is extended, nullifying the squeeze thesis
Last-minute political compromise changes the outcome unpredictably
Procedural signals are deliberately misleading (negotiation tactics)

strategies.name: News Velocity Acceleration Trade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The rate of change of news volume on a topic (news velocity) is more predictive of prediction market price moves than the absolute volume. When news velocity is accelerating (second derivative is positive), the topic is gaining salience and prediction market contracts will re-price. By detecting velocity acceleration before it reaches mainstream attention thresholds, the strategy positions ahead of the crowd. Edge is early detection of emerging narratives before they reach critical mass.
strategies.entry_logic: When news_acceleration exceeds accel_threshold AND the average sentiment of new articles is directionally consistent (positive sentiment for YES contracts, negative for NO), enter in the sentiment direction. Only enter if the contract price has not yet moved more than already_priced_threshold from its 7-day average (to avoid chasing). Larger acceleration = larger position size, capped at max_position_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when news velocity peaks and begins decelerating (first derivative turns negative), as this indicates the narrative has reached maximum attention. Also exit at max_hold_hours or if sentiment reverses. Trail stop at trail_pct.

accel_threshold.name: accel_threshold
accel_threshold.description: Min news acceleration z-score to trigger entry

already_priced_threshold.name: already_priced_threshold
already_priced_threshold.description: Max price move from 7d avg to still enter (avoid chasing)

known_failure_modes: News velocity spike is driven by a single viral but inaccurate article
Sentiment analysis misclassifies sarcasm or nuanced coverage
Market front-runs the news velocity via social media signals

strategies.name: Yield Curve Steepener/Flattener
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The slope of the yield curve (2y-10y spread) oscillates around a mean driven by Fed policy cycle expectations. When the curve is extremely flat or inverted, it tends to steepen as the market prices in rate cuts. When extremely steep, it tends to flatten as rate hike expectations build. By fading extreme curve shapes with duration-neutral steepener/flattener trades, the strategy captures the mean-reversion. Edge is patience and the structural tendency of curve slopes to revert as policy cycles unfold.
strategies.entry_logic: When curve_slope_zscore exceeds entry_zscore (curve too steep): put on a flattener (short 10y, long 2y duration-matched). When below -entry_zscore (curve too flat/inverted): put on a steepener (long 10y, short 2y). Duration-weight the legs to be DV01-neutral. Scale by z-score magnitude.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when curve_slope_zscore returns to within exit_zscore_band. Stop loss at stop_zscore. Also exit if Fed guidance creates a new structural regime for the curve (e.g., explicit forward guidance that anchors the short end).
known_failure_modes: Policy regime change makes historical z-scores irrelevant
Curve shape is driven by structural factors (QE, supply) not cyclical
Roll-down cost on the position erodes profit during holding period

strategies.name: TIPS Breakeven Inflation Trade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The breakeven inflation rate (nominal yield minus TIPS yield) reflects the market's inflation expectation plus a risk premium. When breakevens deviate significantly from model-based inflation forecasts (based on commodity prices, money supply, wage data), the market is mispricing inflation. By buying TIPS and shorting nominals when breakevens are too low (market underpricing inflation), or vice versa, the strategy captures the convergence. Edge is a superior inflation model versus the market's expectation.
strategies.entry_logic: When |breakeven - model_forecast| > entry_spread_threshold, trade the convergence. If breakevens are too low (market underpricing inflation): buy TIPS, short nominals (long breakevens). If too high: short TIPS, buy nominals (short breakevens). Duration-match the legs. Scale by spread magnitude.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when spread narrows to within exit_spread. Stop loss at max_spread (divergence continues). Also exit if model forecast itself shifts to agree with market (model recalibration).

entry_spread_threshold.name: entry_spread_threshold
entry_spread_threshold.description: Min breakeven-vs-model spread (pct points) for entry

max_dv01_usd.name: max_dv01_usd
max_dv01_usd.description: Max DV01 exposure per leg in USD

known_failure_modes: Inflation model is miscalibrated — supply shock creates novel dynamics
TIPS liquidity premium distorts breakevens (technical, not fundamental)
Deflation risk makes long breakevens risky in economic downturns

strategies.name: Interest Rate Swap Spread Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The swap spread (interest rate swap rate minus equivalent maturity Treasury yield) reflects counterparty credit risk, supply/demand for duration, and balance sheet capacity. Extreme swap spread levels mean-revert because the structural drivers (dealer balance sheets, issuance patterns) are cyclical. By trading extreme swap spread levels, the strategy captures the normalization. Edge is that swap spreads exhibit higher mean-reversion reliability than outright rates because the spread components have natural bounds.
strategies.entry_logic: When swap_spread_zscore exceeds entry_zscore, trade the reversion. Wide spreads (high z-score): receive fixed in swap, buy Treasury (spread tightener). Narrow/negative spreads: pay fixed in swap, sell Treasury (spread widener). DV01-neutral construction.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when z-score returns to within exit_zscore. Stop loss at stop_zscore. Time stop at max_hold_months if spread does not normalize (may indicate structural shift).
known_failure_modes: Swap spread driven by regulatory change (Basel requirements) — no reversion
Counterparty credit event widens swap spreads permanently
Low liquidity in one leg makes execution costly

strategies.name: Multi-Factor Value Composite
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Stocks with low valuations relative to fundamentals (low P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA) tend to outperform over 6-18 month horizons. The value premium persists because investors overweight recent growth and glamour, creating a behavioral discount on unfashionable stocks. A composite of multiple value metrics is more robust than any single ratio because it reduces the impact of accounting distortions in individual metrics. Edge is systematic, disciplined harvesting of a well-documented behavioral premium.
strategies.entry_logic: Rank universe by composite_value_score (equal-weight blend of P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA percentile ranks). Go long the cheapest quintile, short the most expensive quintile. Apply sector-neutrality constraint (match sector weights in long and short baskets). Rebalance at rebalance_frequency.
strategies.exit_logic: Rebalance-driven exits when stocks change quintile. Hard exit if composite value score changes by more than rank_change_exit positions within a rebalance period (fundamental deterioration/ improvement). Stop loss at portfolio-level max_drawdown.

quintile_count.name: quintile_count
quintile_count.description: Number of quantile buckets for ranking

known_failure_modes: Value trap — cheap stocks are cheap for fundamental reasons (deteriorating business)
Extended growth outperformance regime makes value underperform
Sector neutrality constraint reduces the signal strength

strategies.name: Quality Factor Long-Only
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: High-quality companies (high ROE, low debt, stable earnings) tend to outperform on a risk-adjusted basis because the market underprices the persistence of quality. Investors anchored on recent trends underestimate the compounding advantage of quality businesses. By systematically overweighting quality stocks and underweighting low-quality stocks, the strategy captures the quality premium. Edge is patient capital allocation aligned with durable competitive advantages.
strategies.entry_logic: Rank universe by composite_quality_score (ROE, low leverage, earnings stability, cash flow quality). Go long top quintile. If long-only, overweight quality and underweight junk relative to benchmark. Rebalance at rebalance_frequency. Apply quality-momentum crossover filter: prefer quality stocks with recent positive momentum.
strategies.exit_logic: Rebalance-driven exits when quality score changes quintile. Exit individual positions if quality deteriorates (ROE drops below min_roe_threshold or leverage increases beyond max_leverage).

min_roe_threshold.name: min_roe_threshold
min_roe_threshold.description: Min ROE to maintain quality status

max_leverage.name: max_leverage
max_leverage.description: Max debt-to-equity to maintain quality status

quality_momentum_weight.name: quality_momentum_weight
quality_momentum_weight.description: Weight of momentum overlay in quality composite

known_failure_modes: Quality factor underperforms during speculative bubbles
Accounting manipulation hides true quality deterioration
High-quality stocks become overpriced, reducing future returns

strategies.name: Analyst Earnings Revision Momentum
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Stocks with upward analyst earnings revisions tend to outperform over the subsequent 1-3 months because revisions reflect new information that the stock price has not fully incorporated. Analysts revise gradually (anchoring bias), meaning a single upward revision often predicts further upward revisions. By buying stocks with the strongest upward revision momentum and shorting those with downward revisions, the strategy captures the post-revision drift. Edge is the predictability of analyst anchoring behavior.
strategies.entry_logic: Rank universe by revision_breadth_ratio (upward minus downward revisions / total revisions). Go long top quintile (net upward revisers), short bottom quintile (net downward revisers). Weight by revision score magnitude. Prefer stocks with low estimate dispersion (analyst agreement on direction). Rebalance at rebalance_frequency.
strategies.exit_logic: Rebalance-driven exits. Exit individual longs if revision turns negative, exit shorts if revision turns positive. Time stop at max_hold_days per position.

min_revision_breadth.name: min_revision_breadth
min_revision_breadth.description: Min revision breadth ratio to include

known_failure_modes: Revisions are front-run by buy-side institutions with earlier access
Revision momentum is a lagging indicator of already-priced information
Herding — analysts revise in the same direction for social reasons, not information

strategies.name: Insider Purchase Signal
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Corporate insider purchases (executives buying their own company stock on the open market) signal undervaluation because insiders have superior information about their company's prospects. Unlike insider sales (which may be for liquidity/diversification), insider buys are almost always informational. Stocks with recent insider buying clusters outperform by 2-5% over the subsequent quarter. Edge is the information asymmetry between insiders and the market, constrained but not eliminated by disclosure requirements.
strategies.entry_logic: When a stock has cluster_score > min_cluster_score (multiple insiders buying within a window), buy. Weight by insider_rank_level (CEO buys more informative than director buys) and buy_value relative to salary. Enter within max_entry_delay_days of the cluster forming. Prefer stocks where insider buying contrasts with negative price action (insiders buying the dip).
strategies.exit_logic: Hold for target_hold_days. Partial exit at 50% of holding period if target_return achieved. Full exit at target_hold_days. Stop loss at stop_loss_pct. Exit early if insiders start selling.

max_entry_delay_days.name: max_entry_delay_days
max_entry_delay_days.description: Max days after cluster to still enter

stop_loss_pct.name: stop_loss_pct
stop_loss_pct.description: Stop loss as pct of entry price

known_failure_modes: Insiders buy before bad news (not all insider purchases are well-timed)
Regulatory filing delay means the market acts before the signal is public
Insider buying is a compensation-related plan, not a conviction signal

strategies.name: Symmetric Spread Market Maker
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: In markets with wide bid-ask spreads and low market maker competition, posting two-sided quotes earns the spread passively. The edge comes from providing a service (liquidity) that earns an economic rent. By maintaining tight inventory limits and rapidly adjusting quotes, adverse selection costs can be kept below the spread earned. This is the canonical market making strategy — profitable when adverse selection is low and spread is wide.
strategies.entry_logic: Post bid and ask orders symmetrically around the estimated fair value at target_half_spread distance. Skew quotes based on inventory: widen the side where inventory is accumulating, tighten the other. Only post when the prevailing spread is above min_spread_to_quote (enough room to be profitable after fees).
strategies.exit_logic: Continuously manage quotes — no discrete exit. Flatten inventory by widening/tightening asymmetrically. Pull all quotes if inventory exceeds max_inventory or if volatility spikes above vol_cutoff. End-of-day flatten at market if needed.

max_inventory.name: max_inventory
max_inventory.description: Max absolute inventory in quote currency

skew_factor.name: skew_factor
skew_factor.description: How much to skew quotes per unit of inventory

vol_cutoff.name: vol_cutoff
vol_cutoff.description: Realized vol threshold to pull quotes

known_failure_modes: Informed flow picks off stale quotes before cancel
Inventory accumulates in one direction during trend
Latency disadvantage against faster market makers

strategies.name: Avellaneda-Stoikov Optimal Quoting
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The Avellaneda-Stoikov framework computes optimal bid-ask quotes that maximize expected terminal wealth given inventory risk aversion, volatility, and time to horizon. By using the model's closed-form reservation price and optimal spread formulas, quotes are dynamically adjusted to balance spread capture against inventory risk. Edge comes from the mathematically optimal trade-off that ad-hoc quoting cannot achieve — quotes tighten when inventory is flat and widen when skewed.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute reservation price = mid - inventory * gamma * sigma^2 * T. Compute optimal spread = gamma * sigma^2 * T + (2/gamma) * ln(1 + gamma/k). Post bid at reservation - spread/2, ask at reservation + spread/2. Refresh quotes continuously as inventory and volatility change.
strategies.exit_logic: No discrete exit — continuous quote management. At time horizon T, flatten any residual inventory at market. Reduce gamma (become more aggressive) as T approaches to clear inventory. Emergency flatten if inventory breaches max_inventory.

horizon_seconds.name: horizon_seconds
horizon_seconds.description: Time horizon for optimization in seconds

max_inventory.name: max_inventory
max_inventory.description: Hard inventory limit triggering emergency flatten

known_failure_modes: Volatility estimate is stale — model quotes are too tight during vol spike
Order arrival rate assumption is wrong — quotes sit unfilled
Model assumes continuous trading — gaps create unhedgeable risk

strategies.name: Cross-Exchange Market Making
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: By market-making on a less liquid exchange while hedging inventory on a more liquid exchange, the strategy captures wide spreads on the illiquid venue while managing directional risk cheaply on the liquid venue. The spread on the illiquid venue exceeds the hedging cost on the liquid venue, creating a net positive expected value. Edge is the willingness to provide liquidity where others won't, hedged by the deeper venue.
strategies.entry_logic: Post two-sided quotes on the illiquid venue with spread set to ensure that after hedge cost on the liquid venue, the net spread exceeds min_net_spread. Use the liquid venue mid-price as fair value reference. Skew quotes based on net inventory across both venues. Limit quote sizes based on hedge capacity on the liquid venue.
strategies.exit_logic: Hedge fills on the illiquid venue immediately with market orders on the liquid venue. If hedge fails (liquid venue moves away), widen quotes on illiquid venue by hedge_fail_spread_increase. Flatten all positions at max_inventory or end of session.

min_net_spread.name: min_net_spread
min_net_spread.description: Minimum net spread after hedge cost

hedge_fail_spread_increase.name: hedge_fail_spread_increase
hedge_fail_spread_increase.description: Additional spread added after hedge failure

known_failure_modes: Hedge venue liquidity disappears during stress — unhedged exposure
Latency spike causes fills without timely hedge
Illiquid venue fills are toxic (informed flow finding the less monitored venue)

strategies.name: News-Aware Dynamic Spread
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Standard market makers get picked off around news events because their quotes are stale relative to the new information. By integrating real-time news classification, the strategy widens spreads or pulls quotes entirely before adverse selection hits. During quiet periods, it tightens spreads to capture more flow. Edge is protecting against the most costly fills while earning more during safe periods.
strategies.entry_logic: Default: post two-sided quotes at base_spread. On news detection (severity > 0), widen spread by severity_multiplier x severity_score. If severity_score > pull_threshold, pull all quotes for pullback_seconds. Resume with gradually narrowing spread as time_since_last_news increases (spread = base + decay_function(time)).
strategies.exit_logic: Continuous quote management. During news pullback, any inventory is held (not panic-sold). After pullback period, resume quoting with wider initial spread that narrows over spread_recovery_seconds.

severity_multiplier.name: severity_multiplier
severity_multiplier.description: Spread multiplier per unit of news severity

pull_threshold.name: pull_threshold
pull_threshold.description: News severity score to pull all quotes

pullback_seconds.name: pullback_seconds
pullback_seconds.description: Seconds to keep quotes pulled after high-severity news

known_failure_modes: News classifier has false negatives — fails to detect material news
Pullback is too slow — fills happen before quotes are pulled
Excessive pulling reduces fill rate below profitability threshold

strategies.name: Active AMM Liquidity Provision
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Concentrated liquidity AMMs (Uniswap v3 style) allow LPs to place liquidity in narrow price ranges. By actively managing the range around the current price (rebalancing as price moves), the LP earns significantly more fees per unit of capital than passive wide-range LPs. The edge is active management — most LPs set ranges and forget, suffering from stale range placement and impermanent loss. Active management captures fees while minimizing impermanent loss through timely range adjustments.
strategies.entry_logic: Deploy liquidity in a range of range_width_pct centered on the current price. Monitor price position within the range. When price moves beyond rebalance_trigger_pct of the range edge, withdraw and redeploy in a new range centered on the current price. Only deploy when estimated fee APR exceeds min_fee_apr.
strategies.exit_logic: Withdraw liquidity entirely when impermanent_loss_estimate exceeds max_il_threshold or when fee APR drops below min_fee_apr. Also withdraw during high-volatility events (detected by rapid price movement). Rebalance (withdraw + redeploy) rather than exit when range drifts.

range_width_pct.name: range_width_pct
range_width_pct.description: Range width as pct of current price

rebalance_trigger_pct.name: rebalance_trigger_pct
rebalance_trigger_pct.description: Position in range triggering rebalance (0.80 = 80% to edge)

min_fee_apr.name: min_fee_apr
min_fee_apr.description: Minimum fee APR to maintain position

known_failure_modes: Gas costs of frequent rebalancing exceed fee income
Sudden price gap moves beyond range before rebalance is possible
MEV bots sandwich the rebalance transactions

strategies.name: Prediction Market Two-Sided Quoting
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market order books are thin relative to traditional markets, creating wide spreads that compensate liquidity providers. By maintaining two-sided quotes with dynamic fair value estimation based on external data feeds (polls, models, news), the strategy earns the spread while staying close to true fair value. Edge is the combination of accurate fair value estimation and willingness to provide persistent liquidity in a market segment where most participants are directional bettors, not market makers.
strategies.entry_logic: Post bid at fair_value - target_half_spread and ask at fair_value + target_half_spread. Widen spread as time_to_resolution decreases (higher gamma near expiry). Skew by inventory. Widen by adverse_selection_estimate (higher AS = wider spread). Pull quotes within pullback_hours of known catalyst events.
strategies.exit_logic: Continuous quoting. Flatten all inventory at least flatten_hours before resolution. If inventory exceeds max_contracts, aggressively price the excess side to shed inventory. Never hold through resolution.

flatten_hours.name: flatten_hours
flatten_hours.description: Hours before resolution to flatten inventory

pullback_hours.name: pullback_hours
pullback_hours.description: Hours around catalyst events to pull quotes

known_failure_modes: Fair value model is wrong — inventory accumulates on the wrong side
Informed traders pick off quotes ahead of resolution-relevant news
Resolution is ambiguous, delaying settlement and trapping inventory

strategies.name: Toxic Flow-Aware Market Maker
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Not all order flow is created equal — some counterparties consistently trade in the direction of future price moves (toxic flow), while others are noise. By classifying incoming order flow in real-time using features like order size, timing relative to events, and counterparty history, the strategy selectively widens quotes or cancels when toxic flow is detected. Quotes stay tight for benign flow. Edge is reduced adverse selection cost, which is the primary source of market-maker losses.
strategies.entry_logic: Default: two-sided quoting at base_spread. Real-time toxicity classifier evaluates each incoming fill. When toxicity_score > widen_threshold, immediately widen spread by toxicity_widen_mult. When VPIN exceeds vpin_pull_threshold, pull all quotes (high overall informed trading probability). Resume normal quoting after cool_down_seconds.
strategies.exit_logic: Continuous. After toxic flow detected, gradually narrow spread back to base over recovery_seconds if no further toxic signal. Flatten inventory acquired during toxic periods at market if unfavorable.

widen_threshold.name: widen_threshold
widen_threshold.description: Toxicity score threshold to widen quotes

toxicity_widen_mult.name: toxicity_widen_mult
toxicity_widen_mult.description: Spread multiplier when toxic flow detected

vpin_pull_threshold.name: vpin_pull_threshold
vpin_pull_threshold.description: VPIN level to pull all quotes

known_failure_modes: Toxicity classifier has false positives — misses benign flow revenue
Toxic flow detection is too slow — fills occur before widen
Counterparty obfuscation (splitting orders) evades detection

strategies.name: Bollinger Band Snapback
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When prices touch or exceed the 2-standard-deviation Bollinger Band on intraday timeframes, the probability of a reversion to the 20-period mean within the next 4-8 bars exceeds 60% in range-bound markets. The edge comes from the tendency of short-term price extremes to overshoot due to stop-loss cascades and momentum chasing, followed by reversion as the transient liquidity imbalance clears.
strategies.entry_logic: When price closes beyond the outer Bollinger Band AND bandwidth is below bandwidth_max_percentile (not in a trending expansion), enter mean-reversion trade. Require RSI confirmation (oversold below 30 for longs, overbought above 70 for shorts). Scale position inversely with bandwidth — tighter bands suggest stronger reversion tendency.
