strategies.exit_logic: Exit at the 20-period moving average (middle band), or at a partial take-profit at 50% reversion. Stop loss at band_extension_mult x the band distance beyond the entry level. Time stop at max_bars.

bandwidth_max_percentile.name: bandwidth_max_percentile
bandwidth_max_percentile.description: Max bandwidth percentile for regime filter

band_extension_mult.name: band_extension_mult
band_extension_mult.description: Stop loss as multiple of band distance

known_failure_modes: Regime transitions from range-bound to trending
Stop-loss cascades extend the move well beyond bands
Low-volatility compression leads to false signals before breakout

strategies.name: Cointegrated Pairs Trading
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Asset pairs that are cointegrated (share a long-run equilibrium relationship, e.g., Coca-Cola/Pepsi, gold miners/gold) have spreads that are mean-reverting by definition. When the spread deviates beyond a threshold, it reverts because the economic relationship that creates the cointegration still holds. Edge is patient capital exploiting statistical mean-reversion that impatient momentum traders create.
strategies.entry_logic: When spread_zscore exceeds entry_zscore AND cointegration_pvalue < max_coint_pvalue (relationship still holds), enter the mean-reversion trade: long the underperformer, short the outperformer at the hedge_ratio. Confirm half-life is within tradeable range (not too slow). Size inversely proportional to half-life.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when spread returns to within exit_zscore of zero. Stop loss if spread widens to stop_zscore (relationship may be breaking). Also exit if cointegration test fails on rolling window (pvalue rises above max_coint_pvalue). Time stop at 3x half_life periods.

max_coint_pvalue.name: max_coint_pvalue
max_coint_pvalue.description: Max cointegration p-value to maintain position

known_failure_modes: Cointegration relationship breaks permanently (structural change)
Half-life increases over time, trapping capital
Both legs move against the position in a correlated sell-off

strategies.name: Overnight Gap Fill
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Equity opening gaps (difference between previous close and current open) are filled within the first 90 minutes of trading approximately 65-70% of the time for gaps < 1%. This occurs because overnight futures positioning creates artificial opening prices that the continuous auction process corrects. Edge is the statistical regularity of gap-fill patterns combined with regime filtering to avoid gap-and-go days.
strategies.entry_logic: At market open, if |gap_size_pct| > min_gap_pct AND |gap_size_pct| < max_gap_pct (not too large — big gaps are news-driven), AND vix_level < max_vix (low fear environment), fade the gap. Enter with limit order placed at the opening price. For gap down: buy. For gap up: sell short.
strategies.exit_logic: Target: previous close (full gap fill). Partial profit at 50% fill. Stop loss at gap_extension_pct beyond the opening price. Time stop at max_hold_minutes — if gap hasn't filled by then, it likely won't.

min_gap_pct.name: min_gap_pct
min_gap_pct.description: Minimum gap size to trigger strategy

max_vix.name: max_vix
max_vix.description: Max VIX level for gap fill to be reliable

max_hold_minutes.name: max_hold_minutes
max_hold_minutes.description: Max minutes to wait for gap fill

known_failure_modes: Gap is driven by genuine overnight news — gap-and-go, no fill
Opening range expansion extends the gap before filling
VIX spikes intraday after entry, invalidating the regime filter

strategies.name: Multi-Timeframe RSI Extreme
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When RSI reaches extreme levels (< 20 or > 80) simultaneously on multiple timeframes (e.g., 5-min and 1-hour), the probability of reversion is significantly higher than single-timeframe signals. Multi-timeframe confluence indicates genuine overextension rather than noise, and the reversion is typically faster and more complete. Edge is the filtering of false signals through timeframe alignment.
strategies.entry_logic: When RSI on at least 2 of 3 timeframes is beyond the oversold/overbought threshold simultaneously, enter the mean reversion. Require the longest timeframe to confirm (not just the shortest, which is noisier). Scale position by the number of confirming timeframes and the ATR percentile (higher vol = smaller size but better entry).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when the shortest timeframe RSI crosses back to neutral (50 level), or at partial targets. Stop loss at stop_atr_mult x ATR beyond entry. Time stop at max_hold_bars on the longest confirming timeframe.

stop_atr_mult.name: stop_atr_mult
stop_atr_mult.description: ATR multiplier for stop loss placement

max_hold_bars.name: max_hold_bars
max_hold_bars.description: Max bars on longest confirming timeframe

known_failure_modes: Multi-timeframe extremes persist in strong trends (RSI can stay overbought)
Flash crash creates extreme RSI across all timeframes but no fill opportunity
ATR-based stops get hit by volatility clustering

strategies.name: VWAP Deviation Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Intraday prices tend to revert to the volume-weighted average price (VWAP) because institutional traders use VWAP as a benchmark. When price deviates significantly from VWAP, the remaining institutional flow for the day acts as a gravitational pull back toward VWAP. Edge comes from the predictability of institutional execution algorithms that target VWAP completion.
strategies.entry_logic: When price deviates from VWAP by more than entry_deviation_pct AND we are in the middle portion of the trading day (not first/last 30 min where VWAP behavior differs), fade toward VWAP. Require cumulative volume to be roughly on track (within volume_tolerance of expected) as a sign of normal institutional activity.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit at VWAP touch, or at partial profit at 50% reversion. Stop loss at 2x the entry deviation from VWAP. Hard exit at session end regardless of position.

entry_deviation_pct.name: entry_deviation_pct
entry_deviation_pct.description: Min price deviation from VWAP to enter

volume_tolerance.name: volume_tolerance
volume_tolerance.description: Max deviation of cumulative volume from expected

stop_mult.name: stop_mult
stop_mult.description: Stop loss as multiple of entry deviation

excluded_session_pct.name: excluded_session_pct
excluded_session_pct.description: Fraction of session to exclude at open/close

known_failure_modes: Large block trade creates persistent VWAP deviation that doesn't revert
News during session shifts VWAP target for remaining flow
Low-volume day makes VWAP less meaningful as a benchmark

strategies.name: Sector Rotation Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When a sector ETF underperforms or outperforms the broad market by an extreme amount over a 5-20 day window, the relative performance tends to mean-revert as the initial catalyst fades and rebalancing flows kick in. This is especially true when the move is not driven by a fundamental regime shift. Edge comes from systematic detection of overextended sector rotations versus genuine sector re-ratings.
strategies.entry_logic: When sector_relative_zscore exceeds entry_zscore AND no recent fundamental surprise explains the move (surprise_flag = false), fade the relative move. Long the underperformer, short SPX (or vice versa). Scale by dispersion level — higher cross-sector dispersion suggests rotation, not just one outlier.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when relative z-score returns to exit_zscore range. Stop loss at stop_zscore (move continues). Time stop at max_hold_days. Exit if fundamental surprise flag flips to true (new information justifies the move).

entry_zscore.name: entry_zscore
entry_zscore.description: Z-score threshold for relative performance entry

known_failure_modes: Sector re-rating is fundamental — no reversion occurs
Rotation extends as macro thesis builds (e.g., rate hike cycle vs growth)
Correlation breakdown makes hedge ineffective

strategies.name: Crypto Intraday Range Mean Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Major crypto assets (BTC, ETH) exhibit strong intraday mean-reversion during low-news periods, with prices oscillating within a range defined by the Asian/European/US session overlap patterns. The 24/7 nature of crypto markets creates identifiable low-activity windows where mean reversion dominates. By identifying the current session's range and fading extremes, the strategy captures the range-bound behavior that accounts for ~70% of non-event trading hours. Edge is regime awareness.
strategies.entry_logic: During identified low-activity sessions (news_activity_score < max_news_activity), when price reaches the upper or lower range_entry_pct of the established session range, fade toward the range midpoint. Only enter if session_range_pct is within min/max range bounds (range is well-defined, not too narrow or wide).
strategies.exit_logic: Target the session range midpoint. Stop loss if price breaks beyond the session extreme by stop_extension_pct (range breakout). Exit at session boundary (when higher-activity session begins). Reduce position if news_activity_score rises above threshold.

range_entry_pct.name: range_entry_pct
range_entry_pct.description: Position within range to trigger entry (0.85 = top/bottom 15%)

max_news_activity.name: max_news_activity
max_news_activity.description: Max news activity score for range regime

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per range trade

known_failure_modes: Unexpected news during low-activity session triggers breakout
Range is too narrow for profitable trading after fees
Session pattern shifts due to macro regime change

strategies.name: Online Regime Classifier Switch
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Markets cycle through distinct regimes (trending, mean-reverting, volatile, quiet) and the optimal strategy differs by regime. An online HMM or random forest classifier trained on volatility, autocorrelation, and flow features can detect regime transitions faster than rule-based approaches. By switching between strategy allocations based on the classified regime, the meta-strategy avoids deploying mean-reversion in trends and vice versa. Edge is adaptive allocation — most systematic strategies use static rules.
strategies.entry_logic: Run the regime classifier on rolling features. When regime posterior probability exceeds transition_threshold for a new regime, reallocate capital across sub-strategies (e.g., 70% trend-following in trending regime, 70% mean-reversion in choppy regime). Transition smoothly over transition_bars to avoid whipsaw. Only act on regime change if confidence exceeds min_regime_confidence.
strategies.exit_logic: No single exit — the meta-strategy reallocates. Individual sub-strategies handle their own exits. The regime switch triggers allocation change, which scales down currently unfavorable strategies over transition_bars.

transition_threshold.name: transition_threshold
transition_threshold.description: Min posterior probability for regime change

min_regime_confidence.name: min_regime_confidence
min_regime_confidence.description: Min confidence to act on regime classification

transition_bars.name: transition_bars
transition_bars.description: Bars over which to smooth allocation transition

known_failure_modes: Regime classifier is too slow — detects regime after it has already shifted
Whipsaw between regimes in transitional periods
Model overfits to recent history and misclassifies novel regimes

strategies.name: Rolling Feature Selection Alpha
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The features that best predict next-period returns change over time. By running rolling feature importance analysis (via gradient boosting or mutual information) and dynamically selecting the top-N features for a lightweight linear model, the strategy adapts to evolving market dynamics. Edge is that static feature sets degrade over time, while adaptive selection captures newly emerging signals and drops decayed ones.
strategies.entry_logic: Every reselection_interval, run feature importance on the full feature universe. Select top_n_features. Fit a simple linear model on the selected features. Generate a prediction score. When |prediction_score| > entry_threshold AND prediction_confidence > min_confidence, enter in the predicted direction. Size proportional to confidence.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when prediction score flips sign, at target_move, or at the next reselection_interval. Stop loss at stop_pct. Exit if rolling OOS Sharpe drops below min_oos_sharpe (model is not performing).

entry_threshold.name: entry_threshold
entry_threshold.description: Min prediction score magnitude for entry

min_oos_sharpe.name: min_oos_sharpe
min_oos_sharpe.description: Min rolling OOS Sharpe to continue trading

known_failure_modes: Feature importance is noisy — selected features are random
Linear model is too simple for the true signal structure
Reselection frequency is too fast (overfitting) or too slow (stale)

strategies.name: Strategy Ensemble Voting
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Individual trading strategies are noisy predictors, but an ensemble of diverse strategies — when they agree on direction — produces a much stronger signal. By requiring a minimum vote consensus among a portfolio of sub-strategies before entering, the meta-strategy filters out noise and only trades high-conviction opportunities. Edge is signal aggregation diversity — errors from different strategies are uncorrelated.
strategies.entry_logic: Collect signals from all N sub-strategies. Weight by recent accuracy. When weighted_consensus_vote_pct exceeds min_consensus_pct (e.g., 70% of strategies agree on direction after accuracy weighting), enter in the consensus direction. Verify that the agreeing strategies are sufficiently diverse (signal_correlation < max_correlation). Size proportional to consensus strength.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when consensus drops below exit_consensus_pct, or when majority of strategies flip to the other direction. Individual sub-strategies may trigger partial exits. Time stop at max_hold_hours.

min_consensus_pct.name: min_consensus_pct
min_consensus_pct.description: Min weighted vote pct for entry

max_correlation.name: max_correlation
max_correlation.description: Max avg correlation among agreeing strategies

known_failure_modes: Sub-strategies are too correlated — ensemble adds no value over single strategy
Consensus is slow to form — entry misses the optimal timing
All strategies fail simultaneously in tail event

strategies.name: Reinforcement Learning Execution Optimizer
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The execution of a given trading signal (order type, timing, splitting, venue selection) significantly impacts realized P&L. A reinforcement learning agent trained to minimize execution cost (slippage + market impact) through trial and error can discover execution policies superior to heuristic TWAP/VWAP algorithms. Edge is learned adaptation to the specific microstructure of each venue and instrument, including patterns too complex for hand-coded rules.
strategies.entry_logic: Given a parent order to execute, the RL agent determines the optimal child order sequence: order type (limit/market), placement level, size per slice, and timing. Agent state includes order book depth, recent trades, and remaining quantity. Actions are limit/market at various aggression levels. Reward is negative implementation shortfall.
strategies.exit_logic: Not applicable — the strategy IS the execution. The parent order's direction comes from another strategy. The RL agent's job is to complete execution by the deadline with minimum cost.

exploration_rate.name: exploration_rate
exploration_rate.description: Epsilon for exploration in live execution

known_failure_modes: RL agent overfits to training period — poor performance in new conditions
Exploration in live trading creates unnecessary losses
Agent learns to exploit simulation artifacts not present in live markets

strategies.name: Cross-Asset Transfer Learning
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Patterns learned in one asset class (e.g., equity mean reversion features) can transfer to another (e.g., prediction market mean reversion) via shared latent structure. By pre-training a neural network on data-rich markets (equities, crypto) and fine-tuning on data-poor markets (prediction markets), the strategy extracts more signal from limited prediction market data than training from scratch. Edge is data efficiency — prediction markets have thin histories, but the underlying behavioral patterns are universal.
strategies.entry_logic: Generate predictions using the transfer-learned model. When |prediction| > entry_threshold AND prediction_confidence > min_confidence AND the fine-tuning loss on target domain is below max_fine_tune_loss (model has adapted), enter in the predicted direction. Weight position by confidence and inverse of fine-tune loss.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when prediction flips sign or confidence drops below exit_confidence. Time stop at max_hold_hours. Exit if fine_tune_loss increases (model is degrading on new data).
known_failure_modes: Negative transfer — source domain patterns don't apply to target
Prediction market microstructure differs too much from source
Fine-tuning overfits to the small target dataset

strategies.name: Unsupervised Anomaly Detector
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Anomalous market microstructure states — combinations of order book, flow, and price features that are statistically unusual — precede significant price moves. An autoencoder or isolation forest trained on "normal" market data flags anomalies that human-designed rules miss. The anomaly itself doesn't predict direction, but combined with a directional indicator (imbalance, sentiment), anomaly detection improves timing. Edge is unsupervised pattern detection that captures novel market states.
strategies.entry_logic: When anomaly_confidence exceeds anomaly_threshold (market state is unusual), check the directional_indicator for trade direction. Only enter if both the anomaly AND a directional signal agree. Higher anomaly confidence = larger position, but cap at max_position_pct. Require time_since_last_anomaly > cooldown_minutes to avoid over-trading during noisy periods.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when anomaly_confidence drops below exit_anomaly_threshold (market returns to normal), at target_move, or at max_hold_minutes. Stop loss at stop_pct.
known_failure_modes: Anomaly detector flags novel but benign market states (false positives)
Model drifts as 'normal' market behavior evolves
Anomalies cluster during crises, creating correlated entries

strategies.name: Bayesian Parameter Adaptation
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Fixed strategy parameters degrade over time as market conditions evolve. By using Bayesian optimization to continuously adapt strategy parameters (entry thresholds, stop levels, position sizes) based on recent performance, the strategy maintains its edge longer than static parameterizations. Edge is extending the half-life of alpha by adapting to the evolving market faster than competitors.
strategies.entry_logic: Run the base strategy with Bayesian-optimized parameters. The optimizer updates parameter posteriors every adaptation_interval based on recent P&L. When the base strategy signals entry (using current adapted parameters), enter. If parameter_stability_score is low (parameters are changing rapidly), reduce position size by instability_discount.
strategies.exit_logic: Follow the base strategy's exit logic using adapted parameters. Additionally exit if parameter_stability_score drops below min_stability (too much parameter uncertainty to trust).

instability_discount.name: instability_discount
instability_discount.description: Position size multiplier when parameters are unstable

exploration_weight.name: exploration_weight
exploration_weight.description: Exploration vs exploitation balance in Bayesian optimization

known_failure_modes: Adaptation chases noise — overfits to recent lucky trades
Parameter space is too large for Bayesian optimization to converge
Underlying strategy alpha has fully decayed — no parameters can help

strategies.name: Order Book Imbalance Momentum
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: A persistent imbalance between bid and ask depth in the order book (significantly more volume on bids than asks or vice versa) predicts short-term price direction. The imbalance reflects supply/demand asymmetry that resolves through price movement. When bid depth significantly exceeds ask depth, price tends to rise as the market clears the thinner ask side. Edge is the speed of detecting and acting on microstructure signals that most participants do not monitor.
strategies.entry_logic: When imbalance_ratio (bid_depth / ask_depth) exceeds imbalance_threshold AND the imbalance has persisted for at least persistence_min_seconds, enter in the direction of the heavier side (long when bids heavy, short when asks heavy). Confirm with aggressor ratio (taker buys dominating = bullish). Enter with limit orders to avoid paying spread.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when imbalance ratio reverts to within neutral_band, or at target_move (a few ticks in the predicted direction). Stop loss if price moves against despite imbalance (imbalance was spoofed or absorbed). Time stop at max_hold_seconds.

imbalance_threshold.name: imbalance_threshold
imbalance_threshold.description: Min bid/ask depth ratio to trigger signal

target_move_bps.name: target_move_bps
target_move_bps.description: Target price move in basis points

known_failure_modes: Order book imbalance is spoofed — large orders are pulled before execution
Imbalance is from a single large resting order, not broad demand
Latency disadvantage means signal is stale by execution time

strategies.name: Spoof Detection Fade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Spoofing — placing large orders with the intent to cancel before execution — creates artificial order book pressure that misleads other participants. By detecting spoofing patterns (large orders that are repeatedly placed and cancelled, or that disappear when price approaches), the strategy fades the implied direction. If a large bid is likely a spoof (intended to attract buyers), the true intent is to sell — so short. Edge is detecting manipulation patterns that most participants take at face value.
strategies.entry_logic: When spoof_confidence_score > min_confidence (order exhibits spoofing characteristics: short lifetime, cancelled when price approaches, repeated placement), fade the spoof direction. If a large bid is classified as spoof, go short (the spoofer wants to sell). Enter quickly with market or aggressive limit orders, as the window is brief.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit within seconds — target a few ticks as the spoof is pulled and price reverts. Hard time stop at max_hold_seconds. Stop loss at stop_ticks if the spoof analysis was wrong and the order is genuine.

min_confidence.name: min_confidence
min_confidence.description: Min spoof detection confidence to act

known_failure_modes: Detection model has false positives — legitimate large orders are faded
Spoofer adapts tactics after being faded repeatedly
Regulatory environment change alters spoofing patterns

strategies.name: Price Level Absorption Signal
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When a large resting order at a key price level (round number, previous high/low) is consistently absorbed — i.e., incoming market orders keep filling against it and it keeps getting replenished — the eventual exhaustion of that level predicts a breakout. The absorption indicates that a large participant is distributing/ accumulating at that level. When the level finally breaks, the price move accelerates. Edge is detecting the absorption pattern and positioning for the breakout before it occurs.
strategies.entry_logic: When a price level shows volume_absorbed exceeding absorbed_threshold AND the level keeps getting tested (replenishment_count > min_replenishments), prepare for breakout. Enter in the direction of the aggressive flow (if aggressive buying is being absorbed by a large resting sell, the breakout will be upward when the sell is exhausted). Use a limit order beyond the absorption level to catch the break.
strategies.exit_logic: Target the move equal to the range compressed during absorption (measured by the time_at_level * typical_atr). Stop loss if price reverses back through the absorption level (false breakout). Time stop at max_hold_minutes.

absorbed_threshold.name: absorbed_threshold
absorbed_threshold.description: Multiple of normal volume absorbed at level

min_replenishments.name: min_replenishments
min_replenishments.description: Min times the level is replenished

known_failure_modes: The resting order is not exhausted — it has unlimited size (iceberg)
False breakout — price breaks the level but reverses immediately
Absorption detection is too slow — breakout happens before entry

strategies.name: VPIN Trade Flow Toxicity
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Volume-Synchronized Probability of Informed Trading (VPIN) measures the imbalance between buy and sell volume in volume-bucketed time. High VPIN readings precede price jumps because they indicate informed flow. By using VPIN as a real-time regime indicator, the strategy enters directional positions aligned with the informed flow direction when VPIN is elevated. Edge is that VPIN captures information aggregation across many small orders that individual order analysis misses.
strategies.entry_logic: When VPIN exceeds vpin_entry_threshold AND the buy/sell imbalance direction is consistent across min_consecutive_buckets, enter in the direction of the dominant flow. Higher VPIN = larger position size (more confidence in informed activity). Enter with limit orders joining the dominant side.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when VPIN drops below vpin_exit_threshold (informed flow subsides) or at target_move_pct. Stop loss at stop_pct. Time stop at max_hold_minutes.

min_consecutive_buckets.name: min_consecutive_buckets
min_consecutive_buckets.description: Min consecutive volume buckets with consistent imbalance

known_failure_modes: VPIN spike is noise (large random order), not informed flow
Volume bucketing parameters are miscalibrated for current market conditions
Informed flow direction reverses mid-trade

strategies.name: Queue Position Priority Scalp
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: In tick-constrained markets (where the minimum price increment is large relative to the spread), queue position at the top of the book is extremely valuable. By maintaining early queue priority through continuous presence, the strategy gets filled before later arrivals and can capture the spread with higher probability. Edge is patience and infrastructure — maintaining queue position requires persistent connectivity and careful order management.
strategies.entry_logic: Place limit orders at the best bid and best ask to join the queue early. Only place on levels where queue_position_estimate indicates front-of-queue status. Maintain presence by not cancelling unless spread changes. When filled on one side, the position becomes directional — immediately hedge with opposite side limit or wait for the other side to fill.
strategies.exit_logic: Target: earn the one-tick spread by getting filled on both sides (buy at bid, sell at ask). If only one side fills, close the directional exposure at break-even or small loss. Never hold a directional position beyond max_hold_seconds.

min_spread_ticks.name: min_spread_ticks
min_spread_ticks.description: Min spread in ticks to be profitable

max_hold_seconds.name: max_hold_seconds
max_hold_seconds.description: Max seconds to hold directional exposure

known_failure_modes: Queue priority is lost due to price level change — must re-enter at back
Adverse selection at front of queue overwhelms spread income
Only one side fills repeatedly — directional losses

strategies.name: Iceberg Order Detection
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Iceberg orders (large orders that only show a small visible portion) are detectable by patterns in the tape: repeated fills at the same price level with the order book instantly refilling. Detecting icebergs reveals the true supply/demand that the order book hides. Trading in the same direction as the iceberg (the large hidden buyer/seller is likely well-informed) captures the informational advantage. Edge is pattern recognition on the tape that most participants miss.
strategies.entry_logic: When iceberg_confidence_score exceeds min_iceberg_confidence (multiple refills at the same price, consistent fill sizes), enter in the iceberg's direction. A hidden buyer (iceberg bids) is bullish. Enter with limit orders slightly behind the iceberg to benefit from its price support. Scale size by iceberg confidence and total absorbed volume.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when the iceberg appears exhausted (refill frequency drops or stops). Also exit at target_ticks profit or stop_ticks loss. Time stop at max_hold_seconds.

min_refills.name: min_refills
min_refills.description: Min refill events to confirm iceberg

known_failure_modes: Iceberg is a trap — placed by an adversary to attract following flow
Iceberg exhausts and price reverses sharply
Detection latency means the iceberg's impact is already priced

strategies.name: Multi-Level Sweep Momentum
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When a large market order sweeps through multiple price levels (consuming all resting liquidity across 3+ levels in rapid succession), it indicates an urgent, likely informed participant. The sweep creates a temporary vacuum in the order book that takes time to refill, and subsequent price discovery tends to continue in the sweep direction. Edge is detecting sweeps in real-time and riding the post-sweep continuation before the book rebuilds.
strategies.entry_logic: When a sweep clears min_levels_swept price levels within max_sweep_duration_ms, enter in the sweep direction immediately. Larger sweeps (more levels, more volume) = larger position. Only enter if post_sweep_book_depth is thin on the continuation side (the vacuum hasn't been filled yet). Use market orders for speed — the edge is in the first seconds post-sweep.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit at target_ticks continuation, or when the order book refills on the continuation side (vacuum filled = momentum over). Hard stop at stop_ticks if sweep was absorbed and price reverses. Time stop at max_hold_seconds.

min_levels_swept.name: min_levels_swept
min_levels_swept.description: Minimum price levels swept to qualify

max_sweep_duration_ms.name: max_sweep_duration_ms
max_sweep_duration_ms.description: Max duration of sweep event in ms

known_failure_modes: Sweep is a fat-finger error — price reverts immediately
Counter-sweep from the other side cancels the vacuum
Latency means entry occurs after the book has already refilled

strategies.name: YES/NO Complement Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When YES + NO prices on the same binary contract deviate from 1.00 (minus fees), buy the underpriced side. Edge comes from temporary liquidity imbalances and slow quote updates on one leg while the other moves. This is a pure structural arbitrage that requires no view on the outcome — only that the sum must converge to 1.00 at settlement.
strategies.entry_logic: Buy both YES and NO when combined ask < 1.00 minus 2x taker fees. Prioritize the leg with deeper liquidity first to minimize leg risk. Only enter when both sides have at least min_depth contracts available at the quoted price.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to resolution for guaranteed profit, or close early if the spread normalizes and the mark-to-market gain exceeds the remaining expected settlement profit net of fees.

min_arb_spread.name: min_arb_spread
min_arb_spread.description: Minimum spread over fees to trigger entry

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max wallet % per position

staleness_threshold_ms.name: staleness_threshold_ms
staleness_threshold_ms.description: Max quote age in ms before quotes are considered stale

min_depth_contracts.name: min_depth_contracts
min_depth_contracts.description: Minimum contracts available at best price on each leg

known_failure_modes: Fee structure change eliminates spread
One leg fills but the other moves away — leg risk
Platform settlement delay creates capital lockup beyond expected horizon

strategies.name: Multi-Bracket Sum Arbitrage
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: For mutually exclusive bracket markets (e.g., GDP growth ranges, temperature bands), the sum of all bracket prices must equal 1.00. When the aggregate ask across all brackets sums to less than 1.00 minus total fees, buying every bracket guarantees a profit at resolution regardless of outcome. Edge persists because market makers on individual brackets price independently and bracket count can be high (5-12), making full-set monitoring rare among retail.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute sum of best asks across all N brackets. If sum < 1.00 - (N * taker_fee), simultaneously buy one contract in each bracket. Fill the deepest/most liquid brackets first to reduce slip. Abort if any bracket moves before all legs fill.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold all brackets to resolution. Exactly one bracket pays 1.00; total cost was < 1.00, so profit is locked in. Early exit only if all brackets can be sold for a combined price exceeding purchase cost plus round-trip fees.

min_sum_discount.name: min_sum_discount
min_sum_discount.description: Minimum discount below 1.00 (after fees) to trigger

max_brackets.name: max_brackets
max_brackets.description: Maximum number of brackets to trade simultaneously

max_leg_slip_pct.name: max_leg_slip_pct
max_leg_slip_pct.description: Max allowed slippage per bracket leg before aborting

fill_timeout_ms.name: fill_timeout_ms
fill_timeout_ms.description: Timeout for completing all bracket fills

known_failure_modes: Partial fills across brackets leave incomplete hedge
Platform adds/removes brackets mid-market, changing the sum constraint
High bracket count multiplies fee drag, eroding thin edges

strategies.name: Cross-Venue Prediction Market Arb
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Identical or economically equivalent contracts trade on multiple prediction market venues (e.g., Kalshi vs Polymarket vs PredictIt). Price discrepancies arise due to different user bases, fee structures, deposit frictions, and information propagation speeds. Buy the cheaper venue and sell the more expensive one. Edge persists because capital is segmented across venues and most participants are single-venue.
strategies.entry_logic: When venue_a_ask < venue_b_bid minus combined fees on both venues, buy on venue A and sell on venue B. Require contract equivalence confidence > 0.95 (same underlying event, same resolution criteria). Execute the less liquid leg first. Apply capital allocation weighted by venue withdrawal speed.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to resolution on both venues. If spread normalizes before resolution, unwind both legs if the mark-to-market profit exceeds remaining settlement profit. Monitor for contract rule divergence between venues.

min_cross_spread.name: min_cross_spread
min_cross_spread.description: Minimum price gap between venues after fees

equivalence_threshold.name: equivalence_threshold
equivalence_threshold.description: Min confidence that contracts resolve identically

max_capital_per_venue_pct.name: max_capital_per_venue_pct
max_capital_per_venue_pct.description: Max portfolio % deployed to any single venue

settlement_lag_tolerance_hours.name: settlement_lag_tolerance_hours
settlement_lag_tolerance_hours.description: Max acceptable settlement timing difference between venues

known_failure_modes: Contract resolution criteria differ subtly between venues
Withdrawal delays on one venue lock up capital, preventing rebalancing
Regulatory action closes one venue mid-trade

strategies.name: Prediction Market Passive Liquidity Provision
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction markets often have wide bid-ask spreads (5-15 cents) due to low market maker competition and retail-dominated flow. By posting two-sided quotes inside the prevailing spread with appropriate skew based on fair value estimates, the strategy earns the spread while managing adverse selection from informed flow around event catalysts. Edge comes from providing a service (liquidity) that is undersupplied.
strategies.entry_logic: Post limit orders on both sides of the estimated fair value. Skew quotes toward the side with less adverse selection risk. Widen spread as event resolution approaches (adverse selection increases). Pull quotes entirely within final_pullback_hours of a known catalyst. Minimum spread must exceed 2x taker fees to be profitable.
strategies.exit_logic: Continuously manage inventory by adjusting quote placement. Flatten inventory when net position exceeds max_inventory_tilt or when approaching event resolution. Accept P&L and exit all positions at least final_pullback_hours before resolution.

target_half_spread.name: target_half_spread
target_half_spread.description: Target half-spread from fair value in cents

max_inventory_tilt.name: max_inventory_tilt
max_inventory_tilt.description: Max net directional contracts before flattening

skew_sensitivity.name: skew_sensitivity
skew_sensitivity.description: How aggressively to skew quotes based on flow imbalance

final_pullback_hours.name: final_pullback_hours
final_pullback_hours.description: Hours before resolution to pull all quotes and flatten

known_failure_modes: Informed flow picks off quotes ahead of news — adverse selection
Sudden liquidity withdrawal by competitors widens spreads beyond model
Event resolution is ambiguous, creating long settlement delays with capital locked

strategies.name: Scheduled Event Catalyst Fade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market prices systematically overshoot in the 1-4 hours following a scheduled catalyst (earnings, economic data release, court ruling). Retail participants over-extrapolate early signals (e.g., initial headline) before the full context is digested. Fading the initial move after a minimum reaction window captures the mean reversion as sophisticated participants correct the overshoot. Edge is behavioral — anchoring and availability bias.
strategies.entry_logic: After a scheduled event resolves and the contract price moves more than overshoot_threshold from its pre-event level within the reaction_window, take the opposite position. Only enter if the event type historically shows > 60% mean reversion rate in the post-reaction period. Scale position size inversely with move magnitude (larger moves = smaller positions due to higher chance of genuine information).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price retraces reversion_target_pct of the initial move, or at max_hold_hours, whichever comes first. Stop loss at 1.5x the initial move magnitude (i.e., the move extends further against the fade).

overshoot_threshold.name: overshoot_threshold
overshoot_threshold.description: Minimum price move to classify as overshoot

reaction_window_minutes.name: reaction_window_minutes
reaction_window_minutes.description: Minutes to wait after event before entering fade

reversion_target_pct.name: reversion_target_pct
reversion_target_pct.description: Target retracement as pct of initial move

max_hold_hours.name: max_hold_hours
max_hold_hours.description: Maximum hours to hold the fade position

known_failure_modes: The move is genuine information, not overshoot — fade loses
Liquidity dries up after event, making exit costly
Multiple cascading events prevent reversion

strategies.name: Prediction Market Term Structure Roll
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When the same underlying event has contracts expiring at different dates (e.g., "Will X happen by March?" vs "Will X happen by June?"), the price difference between tenors should follow a monotonic relationship reflecting cumulative probability. When this relationship inverts or the term premium deviates from its historical distribution, trade the convergence. Edge comes from fragmented attention — most traders focus on a single tenor and ignore the term structure.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute term premium = far_tenor_price - near_tenor_price. When term_premium z-score exceeds entry_zscore_threshold (premium too high), buy near and sell far. When z-score is below negative threshold (premium too compressed or inverted), sell near and buy far. Size proportional to z-score magnitude capped at max_position_pct.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when term premium z-score returns to within exit_zscore_band of zero, or when the near-dated contract approaches expiry (within rollover_days). Stop out if z-score reaches 2x entry level (divergence accelerating).

exit_zscore_band.name: exit_zscore_band
exit_zscore_band.description: Z-score band around zero to trigger exit

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per term structure trade

rollover_days.name: rollover_days
rollover_days.description: Days before near-expiry to force exit

known_failure_modes: Term structure reflects genuine new information, not noise
Near-dated contract resolves unexpectedly, leaving naked far-dated exposure
Insufficient liquidity in one tenor makes the spread untradeable

strategies.name: Correlated Contract Basket Divergence
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Groups of prediction market contracts share common drivers (e.g., election contracts for candidates in the same race, weather contracts for the same region). When one contract in a correlated basket moves without the others adjusting, the lagging contracts are mispriced. Buy/sell the lagging contracts in the direction implied by the leader. Edge is informational — participants in one contract may not monitor related contracts.
strategies.entry_logic: Identify leader-laggard pairs within the basket using rolling correlation and lead-lag analysis. When leader moves beyond lead_threshold and laggard has not moved proportionally (residual > residual_threshold), trade laggard in the expected direction. Weight position by correlation strength and historical lead-lag reliability.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when laggard catches up (residual returns to within convergence_band), or at max_hold_hours. If correlation structure breaks (rolling correlation drops below min_correlation), exit immediately as the relationship may have changed.

lead_threshold.name: lead_threshold
lead_threshold.description: Minimum leader move to trigger signal

min_correlation.name: min_correlation
min_correlation.description: Minimum rolling correlation to maintain position

max_hold_hours.name: max_hold_hours
max_hold_hours.description: Maximum hold time for convergence trade

known_failure_modes: Correlation breaks permanently — contracts are less related than assumed
Leader move was noise, not information — both legs mean revert
Laggard has fundamental reason to diverge (unique news for that contract)

strategies.name: Resolution Day Pinning / Gamma Scalp
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: In the final 24-48 hours before resolution, binary contract prices become highly convex — small changes in the underlying probability cause large price swings. Contracts near 0.50 exhibit maximum gamma. By rapidly scalping small mean reversions around the current price level during this high-gamma window, the strategy profits from the increased realized volatility relative to the implied vol embedded in the spread. Edge is analogous to options gamma scalping near expiry.
strategies.entry_logic: Within the final gamma_window_hours before resolution, when contract price is within pin_range of 0.50, enter mean-reversion scalps. Buy when price dips below fair value by scalp_threshold; sell when price rises above by the same amount. Increase position frequency as gamma increases (closer to resolution and closer to 0.50).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit each micro-position at fair value or at scalp_threshold profit. Hard stop at max_drawdown_per_scalp. Cease all trading and flatten when the resolution outcome becomes deterministic (price moves to 0.95+ or 0.05-).

gamma_window_hours.name: gamma_window_hours
gamma_window_hours.description: Hours before resolution to activate strategy

scalp_threshold.name: scalp_threshold
scalp_threshold.description: Price deviation from fair value to trigger scalp

pin_range.name: pin_range
pin_range.description: Max distance from 0.50 for strategy activation

max_drawdown_per_scalp.name: max_drawdown_per_scalp
max_drawdown_per_scalp.description: Stop loss per individual scalp trade

known_failure_modes: Outcome becomes clear rapidly — price gaps from 0.50 to 0.95 with no scalp opportunity
Spread widens near expiry as market makers pull back, eroding scalp edge
High-frequency informed flow systematically picks off scalp orders

strategies.name: Anchoring Bias Drift
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market prices systematically under-react to incremental information when no single catalyst is large enough to grab attention. Participants anchor to the current price and adjust insufficiently — a well-documented behavioral bias. By tracking the cumulative delta of small positive or negative information signals and comparing to the observed price change, the strategy identifies contracts that should have moved more than they have. Edge is behavioral and persists because retail participants dominate prediction market flow.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute anchoring_gap = expected_price_move (from cumulative info signals) minus actual_price_move. When |anchoring_gap| > gap_threshold, buy if gap is positive (price too low) or sell if negative (price too high). Require at least min_info_events information signals in the lookback window to ensure the cumulative delta is statistically meaningful.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price converges to within convergence_pct of the model's fair value, or at max_hold_days. Partial profit-taking at 50% of expected convergence. Stop loss if new information reverses the cumulative delta direction.

gap_threshold.name: gap_threshold
gap_threshold.description: Minimum anchoring gap to trigger entry

min_info_events.name: min_info_events
min_info_events.description: Minimum info events in lookback window

convergence_pct.name: convergence_pct
convergence_pct.description: Price distance from model fair value to exit

known_failure_modes: Cumulative information model is miscalibrated — price is actually correct
Market regime shifts make historical anchoring estimates unreliable
Contract approaches resolution before convergence, increasing binary risk

strategies.name: Volume-Confirmed Breakout
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Large prediction market volume surges accompanied by sustained directional price movement indicate informed flow entering the market. Unlike in equities where volume signals are noisy, prediction market volume is sparse enough that a 3-5x volume spike is highly informative. Following the direction of volume-confirmed price breakouts captures the information advantage of early informed participants. Edge decays as the information diffuses, but the initial move typically captures 60-70% of the total price adjustment.
strategies.entry_logic: When rolling_volume_zscore exceeds volume_surge_threshold AND price has moved in a consistent direction (>= direction_consistency_pct of trades on one side), enter in the direction of the flow. Require the bid-ask spread to not have widened more than max_spread_widening (to avoid entering into a liquidity vacuum). Enter with limit orders placed at the current best bid/ask to avoid paying the full spread.
strategies.exit_logic: Trail a stop at trail_pct below the high-water mark. Take profit if position reaches 3x the initial spread cost. Time-stop at max_hold_hours as the information edge decays. Exit immediately if volume reverses direction (reversal_volume_ratio of flow flips).

volume_surge_threshold.name: volume_surge_threshold
volume_surge_threshold.description: Z-score threshold for volume surge detection

direction_consistency_pct.name: direction_consistency_pct
direction_consistency_pct.description: Min pct of volume on one side to confirm direction

trail_pct.name: trail_pct
trail_pct.description: Trailing stop distance as pct of price

max_hold_hours.name: max_hold_hours
max_hold_hours.description: Maximum hold time before forced exit

known_failure_modes: Volume surge is wash trading or self-dealing, not informed flow
Information is already fully priced in by the time volume is detected
Breakout reverses as initial flow was a large retail order, not informed

strategies.name: Social Media Mention Surge
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Sudden spikes in social media mentions of a prediction market topic (measured across Twitter/X, Reddit, and news aggregators) precede price moves by 15-60 minutes. The lag exists because most prediction market participants do not have real-time social monitoring. By detecting mention surges above the rolling baseline and classifying directional sentiment, the strategy positions before the crowd arrives. Edge is speed of social signal processing.
strategies.entry_logic: When mention_count_zscore > surge_threshold AND weighted_sentiment is directionally consistent (magnitude > sentiment_min_magnitude), enter in the sentiment direction. Prioritize surges driven by influencer accounts (influencer_ratio > 0.3) over pure retail chatter. Only enter if contract price has not already moved more than max_already_moved from pre-surge baseline.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when mention velocity decelerates (peak attention has passed), or at max_hold_hours. Trail stop at trail_pct. Exit immediately if sentiment reverses (new wave of opposite sentiment emerges).

surge_threshold.name: surge_threshold
surge_threshold.description: Z-score threshold for mention surge detection

sentiment_min_magnitude.name: sentiment_min_magnitude
sentiment_min_magnitude.description: Min absolute sentiment score to confirm direction

max_already_moved.name: max_already_moved
max_already_moved.description: Max price move since surge start to still enter

known_failure_modes: Bot-driven mention surge creates false signal
Sentiment classification fails on sarcasm or irony
Social media platform API rate limits cause data gaps

strategies.name: Fear/Greed Index Contrarian
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Composite fear/greed indices (aggregating VIX, put/call ratios, social sentiment, safe haven flows) reach extreme readings when retail positioning is maximally one-sided. At extremes, the crowded trade is most vulnerable to reversal. By fading extreme fear (buying) and extreme greed (selling) in prediction markets correlated with broad risk sentiment, the strategy captures mean reversion driven by behavioral overextension. Edge is patience — most participants are momentum followers at extremes.
strategies.entry_logic: When fear_greed_percentile exceeds extreme_threshold (extreme greed) or falls below (100 - extreme_threshold) (extreme fear), enter contrarian to the prevailing sentiment. In extreme fear: buy risk-on prediction market contracts. In extreme greed: sell or buy risk-off contracts. Scale size inversely with how far into the extreme the reading is. Require the extreme to persist for min_persistence_days to filter whipsaws.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when fear/greed index returns to neutral zone (between 35-65 percentile). Partial exit at halfway point. Stop loss if the extreme extends to historic levels (beyond stop_percentile) — sometimes extremes get more extreme.

min_persistence_days.name: min_persistence_days
min_persistence_days.description: Days the extreme must persist before entry

stop_percentile.name: stop_percentile
stop_percentile.description: Percentile beyond which to stop out

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct for contrarian position

known_failure_modes: Regime change makes the fear/greed index poorly calibrated
Extreme extends far beyond historical norms (tail event)
Mean reversion takes longer than capital can wait

strategies.name: Narrative Regime Shift Detector
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Media narratives around prediction market topics undergo regime shifts — the dominant framing changes from bullish to bearish or vice versa. These shifts are detectable via NLP topic modeling before they fully impact prices because narrative changes propagate through media outlets over 12-48 hours. By detecting when the topic cluster distribution shifts significantly from its recent baseline, the strategy anticipates the price impact of the new narrative. Edge is early detection of framing changes before they reach critical mass.
strategies.entry_logic: When KL divergence between current topic distribution and baseline exceeds shift_threshold AND the new dominant narrative has clear directional sentiment (magnitude > min_sentiment_magnitude), enter in the direction of the new narrative. Weight by shift confidence (higher confidence = larger position). Only enter if the contract price has not already reflected the narrative (price-narrative divergence > price_lag_threshold).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price-narrative divergence closes (price catches up to narrative implication), or when another narrative shift occurs. Time stop at max_hold_hours. Trail stop at trail_pct.

shift_threshold.name: shift_threshold
shift_threshold.description: KL divergence threshold for narrative shift detection

min_sentiment_magnitude.name: min_sentiment_magnitude
min_sentiment_magnitude.description: Min sentiment magnitude of new narrative

known_failure_modes: Topic model is unstable and detects false regime shifts
Narrative shift is temporary — reverts within hours
Price leads narrative (smart money already positioned)

strategies.name: Whisper Number Consensus Fade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: For economic and earnings-related prediction markets, an unofficial "whisper number" emerges from aggregating buy-side analyst expectations, options positioning, and social media consensus. This whisper number often diverges from the official consensus estimate. When the prediction market price aligns with the whisper rather than the official consensus, and the whisper historically has a particular hit rate, the strategy trades the mispricing between the two. Edge comes from systematically tracking whisper vs official consensus accuracy across event types.
strategies.entry_logic: When |whisper - official| > whisper_gap_threshold AND market price aligns with one estimate over the other, trade toward the estimate that has higher historical accuracy for this event type. If historical accuracy favors official but market tracks whisper, fade the market toward official (and vice versa). Only enter within entry_window_days of the event.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold through event resolution. Early exit only if new information causes both whisper and official to converge. No stop loss on the position itself — risk managed through position sizing (max_position_pct).

whisper_gap_threshold.name: whisper_gap_threshold
whisper_gap_threshold.description: Min gap between whisper and official estimates

min_historical_accuracy_edge.name: min_historical_accuracy_edge
min_historical_accuracy_edge.description: Min accuracy advantage of one estimate over the other

entry_window_days.name: entry_window_days
entry_window_days.description: Days before event to enter position

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per whisper trade

known_failure_modes: Whisper number source is unreliable or manipulated
Event type has insufficient history for accuracy comparison
Both estimates are wrong — actual outcome is an outlier

strategies.name: Polymarket Comment Thread Sentiment
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Polymarket and similar platforms have comment sections where informed participants share analysis. The aggregate sentiment of highly-rated comments (by other participants) contains alpha that is not yet fully reflected in the market price, because most traders do not systematically read and quantify comment-level analysis. By scraping, scoring, and aggregating comment sentiment with quality weighting, the strategy extracts crowd wisdom that is superior to the price alone. Edge is structured information extraction from unstructured text.
strategies.entry_logic: Compute quality-weighted comment sentiment (upvoted/liked comments get higher weight). When the comment-implied fair value diverges from market price by more than entry_divergence AND comment volume is above min_comment_volume (enough signal), enter toward the comment-implied direction. Higher divergence = larger position.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when market price converges to comment-implied value, or at max_hold_hours. Exit if new comments shift the aggregate sentiment by more than sentiment_reversal_threshold. Trail stop at trail_pct.

sentiment_reversal_threshold.name: sentiment_reversal_threshold
sentiment_reversal_threshold.description: Sentiment shift to trigger reversal exit

known_failure_modes: Comment section is astroturfed by coordinated accounts
High-rated commenters are wrong (echo chamber effect)
Sentiment parsing fails on prediction-market-specific jargon

strategies.name: Attention Half-Life Mean Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market prices overshoot when a topic is at peak attention and then mean-revert as attention decays. Each topic category has a characteristic attention half-life (political scandals: ~3 days, natural disasters: ~5 days, corporate controversies: ~7 days). By modeling the attention decay curve after peak and comparing the current price to the model-implied fair value given current attention level, the strategy captures the overshoot premium. Edge is quantitative modeling of a well-known but rarely formalized behavioral pattern.
strategies.entry_logic: After attention has peaked (detected by attention level declining for min_decay_days), compare market price to the decay model's predicted fair value. If |market - model| > entry_threshold, fade the market toward the model. Only enter if attention has declined by at least min_attention_decline_pct from peak.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price converges to model-predicted value, or when a new attention spike occurs (topic becomes newsworthy again). Time stop at 2x the topic category half-life. Stop loss at stop_loss_pct.

min_decay_days.name: min_decay_days
min_decay_days.description: Min days of attention decline to confirm peak

min_attention_decline_pct.name: min_attention_decline_pct
min_attention_decline_pct.description: Min attention decline from peak to enter

stop_loss_pct.name: stop_loss_pct
stop_loss_pct.description: Stop loss as pct of position

known_failure_modes: New development causes attention resurgence (second wave)
The price overshoot was actually correct — new equilibrium, not noise
Half-life model is miscalibrated for novel topic types

strategies.name: Expert Disagreement Volatility Buyer
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When domain experts publicly disagree about a prediction market topic (measured by variance in expert opinion signals), realized outcome volatility increases. Prediction markets in this state tend to underestimate the probability of extreme outcomes because the price settles at a compromise midpoint. By buying both tails (far YES and far NO for bracket markets, or cheap near-zero/near-one contracts), the strategy profits when one extreme materializes. Edge is that expert disagreement signals genuine uncertainty that the market consensus underprices.
strategies.entry_logic: When expert_opinion_variance exceeds disagreement_threshold AND the number of tracked experts exceeds min_experts, buy tail contracts (contracts priced below tail_price_threshold or above 1 - threshold). Allocate equally between both tails. Only enter if the implied binary vol is below the model-implied vol given the disagreement level (market is too complacent).
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to resolution. Early exit only if expert disagreement converges significantly (variance drops below exit_variance). No directional stop loss — the trade is long volatility, so one side is expected to lose.

disagreement_threshold.name: disagreement_threshold
disagreement_threshold.description: Min expert opinion variance to trigger entry

min_experts.name: min_experts
min_experts.description: Minimum experts tracked for signal validity

tail_price_threshold.name: tail_price_threshold
tail_price_threshold.description: Max price for a tail contract to be considered cheap

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct across both tails

known_failure_modes: Experts disagree but outcome is boring (middle ground prevails)
Tail contract liquidity is too thin to fill at model prices
Expert tracking source has selection bias

strategies.name: Google Trends Breakout Signal
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Google search trends for prediction market topics lead price changes by 6-24 hours because search behavior reflects emerging public interest before it translates into trading activity. A breakout in search volume (relative to baseline) for a specific contract topic predicts increased trading volume and directional price pressure. Edge is that Google Trends data is public but rarely monitored systematically by prediction market participants.
strategies.entry_logic: When trends_breakout_zscore > breakout_threshold AND search query analysis suggests directional intent (search_sentiment_proxy magnitude > min_query_sentiment), enter in the implied direction. Higher breakout z-score = larger position. Only enter if contract has not already moved more than max_already_priced since the search breakout started.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when search interest peaks and declines (trends index drops below peak by decay_exit_pct), or at max_hold_hours. Trail stop at trail_pct. Exit if trends breakout reverses (z-score drops below 1.0).

breakout_threshold.name: breakout_threshold
breakout_threshold.description: Z-score threshold for search volume breakout

decay_exit_pct.name: decay_exit_pct
decay_exit_pct.description: Trends decline from peak to trigger exit

known_failure_modes: Search spike is driven by a viral meme, not genuine information seeking
Google Trends data has latency or sampling artifacts
The contract is too illiquid for the predicted volume to materialize

strategies.name: LLM Consensus Probability Extraction
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Large language models, when prompted with all publicly available information about a prediction market question, produce probability estimates that aggregate diverse information sources more comprehensively than any individual market participant. By running multiple LLM inference calls with varied prompts and temperature settings, generating a consensus probability distribution, and comparing it to the market price, the strategy identifies mispriced contracts. Edge is that LLMs process breadth of information that no human can replicate manually, and most prediction market participants do not use systematic LLM-based analysis.
strategies.entry_logic: Run N inference calls (varied temperature, prompts) and compute the median and variance of LLM probability estimates. When |llm_consensus - market_price| > entry_threshold AND llm_variance < max_llm_variance (LLM is confident), enter in the LLM's direction. Scale position by divergence magnitude and inverse of LLM variance.
strategies.exit_logic: Re-run LLM analysis daily. Exit when divergence closes or LLM consensus shifts to agree with market. Time stop at max_hold_days. Exit if LLM variance increases significantly (new ambiguous info).

max_llm_variance.name: max_llm_variance
max_llm_variance.description: Max LLM estimate variance for confidence

num_inference_calls.name: num_inference_calls
num_inference_calls.description: Number of LLM inference runs for consensus

known_failure_modes: LLM training data cutoff makes it unaware of recent developments
LLM hallucinates facts that bias the probability estimate
Market is pricing non-public information the LLM cannot access

strategies.name: Insider Activity Proxy Signal
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Unusually large or well-timed trades on prediction market contracts, especially by accounts with high historical accuracy, serve as a proxy for insider or expert knowledge. By tracking wallet/account-level P&L history and flagging when high-accuracy accounts take new large positions, the strategy piggybacks on informed flow. Edge is systematic tracking of account-level performance data that most participants ignore.
strategies.entry_logic: When a large trade (size > large_trade_threshold percentile) is detected from an account with historical accuracy > min_accuracy AND the position is notably larger than their typical size (size_vs_history_ratio > size_surprise_threshold), follow the direction. Enter within max_follow_delay_minutes to capture the information before it diffuses. Position size proportional to trader's accuracy track record.
strategies.exit_logic: Trail stop at trail_pct. Exit at max_hold_hours or when the informed trader closes their position. Exit immediately if the same trader reverses direction.

min_accuracy.name: min_accuracy
min_accuracy.description: Min historical accuracy of tracked account

size_surprise_threshold.name: size_surprise_threshold
size_surprise_threshold.description: Min ratio of trade size vs account's typical size

max_follow_delay_minutes.name: max_follow_delay_minutes
max_follow_delay_minutes.description: Max minutes after detection to still enter

known_failure_modes: High-accuracy account has a losing streak — past performance not predictive
Large trade is hedging an off-platform position, not a directional signal
Tracker accounts detect the following and front-run with bait trades

strategies.name: Dual Moving Average Crossover
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When a fast moving average crosses above a slow moving average, it confirms that recent price action is stronger than the longer-term trend — indicating a new uptrend. The system captures the middle portion of trends by entering after confirmation and exiting when the crossover reverses. Edge is trend persistence — asset prices exhibit positive serial correlation at multi-day horizons due to gradual information diffusion and behavioral herding.
strategies.entry_logic: Enter long when fast_ma crosses above slow_ma AND ADX > min_adx (trend strength filter). Enter short when fast_ma crosses below slow_ma AND ADX > min_adx. Only trade in the direction of the 200-day MA when direction_filter is enabled.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when the crossover reverses (fast crosses back through slow). Trail stop at trail_atr_mult x ATR below the highest high since entry (for longs). Time stop at max_hold_days to prevent stale positions in flat markets.

min_adx.name: min_adx
min_adx.description: Minimum ADX for trend strength filter

known_failure_modes: Whipsaw in choppy markets — repeated false crossovers
Entry lag causes the strategy to miss early trend profits
ADX filter removes valid signals during mild trends

strategies.name: ATR Channel Breakout
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: When price breaks above/below a channel defined by N-day high/low plus an ATR buffer, it signals the start of a new trend. The ATR buffer reduces false breakouts caused by normal volatility. Edge comes from the empirical observation that breakouts from low-volatility consolidation zones precede the largest trending moves, as compressed ranges indicate building energy.
strategies.entry_logic: Enter long when price closes above n_day_high + atr_buffer * ATR. Enter short when price closes below n_day_low - atr_buffer * ATR. Require volume_on_breakout_bar > min_volume_mult * average volume (volume confirmation). Position size = risk_pct / (atr_stop_mult * ATR).
strategies.exit_logic: Trailing stop at atr_stop_mult * ATR below highest close since entry (for longs). Take partial profit at 2R (2x initial risk). Exit if price re-enters the channel (false breakout).
known_failure_modes: False breakout — price breaks channel then reverses immediately
Gap-driven breakout leads to unfavorable entry price
ATR buffer is too large for the asset, missing valid breakouts

strategies.name: Cross-Asset Momentum Rotation
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Assets that have performed well over the past 3-12 months tend to continue outperforming over the next 1-3 months (time-series and cross-sectional momentum). By ranking a universe of assets by their risk-adjusted momentum and going long the top quintile and short the bottom quintile, the strategy captures the momentum premium. Edge is the well-documented behavioral basis: investors underreact to information, creating persistent trends.
strategies.entry_logic: Rank universe by 12-1 momentum (12-month return excluding the most recent month). Go long the top quintile, short the bottom quintile. Apply absolute momentum filter: only go long if the asset's return is positive, only short if negative. Rebalance at rebalance_frequency intervals. Equal weight within quintiles.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit positions at rebalance when rank changes quintile. Also exit longs if absolute momentum turns negative, and shorts if absolute momentum turns positive. Crash protection: exit all if broad market drawdown exceeds crash_exit_threshold (momentum crash risk).

skip_months.name: skip_months
skip_months.description: Months to skip (most recent) to avoid reversal

crash_exit_threshold.name: crash_exit_threshold
crash_exit_threshold.description: Broad market drawdown to trigger full exit

known_failure_modes: Momentum crash — past winners reverse sharply during market stress
Crowded momentum trade leads to simultaneous unwinds
Transaction costs from frequent rebalancing erode returns

strategies.name: Adaptive Turtle Breakout
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The classic Turtle Trading system (20-day and 55-day channel breakouts) has decayed in traditional markets but remains viable in less efficient markets (crypto, prediction markets) where trend-following is under-deployed. By adapting the Turtle parameters dynamically based on recent volatility regime, the strategy captures trends while reducing whipsaw in choppy periods. Edge is applying a proven framework to inefficient markets with adaptive parameters.
strategies.entry_logic: Enter long on close above adaptive_channel_period high. Enter short on close below adaptive_channel_period low. Channel period adapts: shorter in trending regimes, longer in choppy. Add to position (pyramid) every 0.5 ATR in profit, up to max_units. Position unit = risk_pct / (2 * ATR).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit on opposite channel breakout (shorter exit channel of exit_channel_period). Stop loss at 2 * ATR from entry for each unit. If max_units reached and price stalls, tighten stops to 1 * ATR.

risk_pct.name: risk_pct
risk_pct.description: Risk per unit as pct of portfolio

known_failure_modes: Pyramiding amplifies losses during false breakouts
Adaptive parameter selection adds overfitting risk
Crypto-specific risks (exchange issues) compound with leveraged positions

strategies.name: Prediction Market Directional Drift
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Prediction market contracts exhibit short-term momentum — a contract that has moved directionally over the past 24-72 hours tends to continue in the same direction. This occurs because information diffuses slowly through the prediction market participant base, and early movers create a self-reinforcing cycle as others observe the price movement and pile on. Edge is capturing the drift during the information diffusion phase before the market reaches equilibrium.
strategies.entry_logic: When momentum_zscore > entry_momentum_threshold AND volume is confirming (volume_trend_direction matches price direction), enter in the momentum direction. Avoid contracts within close_to_resolution days of resolution (binary risk dominates momentum). Scale position inversely with days_to_resolution (more time = more room for drift).
strategies.exit_logic: Trail stop at trail_pct. Exit when momentum_zscore drops below exit_momentum_threshold. Time stop at max_hold_days. Exit if volume reverses direction (momentum exhaustion signal).

close_to_resolution_days.name: close_to_resolution_days
close_to_resolution_days.description: Days to resolution below which to avoid entry

trail_pct.name: trail_pct
trail_pct.description: Trailing stop as pct of price

known_failure_modes: Momentum is a dead-cat bounce — reversal follows
Approaching resolution creates binary risk overwhelming momentum
Low liquidity means momentum is driven by single participant

strategies.name: Relative Strength Index Trend Filter
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Stocks with high relative strength versus their sector peers (top decile 3-month relative performance) tend to continue outperforming for 2-4 weeks, especially when accompanied by improving fundamentals (upward earnings revisions). The dual filter of price momentum and fundamental momentum reduces false signals from pure speculative runs. Edge is the interaction effect between price and fundamental momentum, which is stronger than either alone.
strategies.entry_logic: When relative_strength_vs_sector is in the top decile AND earnings_revision_direction is positive AND price is above the 50-day MA, go long. Position size is equal-weighted across qualifying names, capped at max_positions. Rebalance weekly, dropping names that fall below top quartile.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when relative strength drops below the top quartile, or when earnings revision turns negative. Trail stop at trail_atr_mult x ATR. Hard exit at max_hold_weeks.

rs_decile_threshold.name: rs_decile_threshold
rs_decile_threshold.description: Top N deciles for relative strength (1 = top 10%)

known_failure_modes: Sector rotation causes top relative strength names to underperform suddenly
Earnings revision data is stale or manipulated
Concentration in few names that gap down on bad news

strategies.name: Volatility Risk Premium Harvest
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Implied volatility systematically exceeds realized volatility because option buyers pay a premium for insurance (the variance risk premium). By systematically selling options (straddles, strangles) and delta-hedging, the strategy harvests this premium. The VRP averages 2-4 vol points annually and is one of the most persistent risk premia in financial markets. Edge is the willingness to bear volatility risk that option buyers are eager to transfer.
strategies.entry_logic: When vrp_current (IV minus realized) exceeds min_vrp_threshold AND vrp_percentile is above vrp_percentile_min (premium is richer than usual), sell delta-neutral straddles at the nearest monthly expiry. Delta-hedge daily. Prefer strikes near ATM for maximum theta capture. Scale size by VRP magnitude.
strategies.exit_logic: Hold to expiry or close when VRP turns negative (realized exceeds implied — premium has evaporated). Stop loss at max_loss_pct on the straddle position. Roll to next month if holding to expiry.

min_vrp_threshold.name: min_vrp_threshold
min_vrp_threshold.description: Min variance risk premium (vol points) to enter

vrp_percentile_min.name: vrp_percentile_min
vrp_percentile_min.description: Min VRP percentile rank for entry

max_loss_pct.name: max_loss_pct
max_loss_pct.description: Max loss on straddle before closing

known_failure_modes: Realized vol explodes beyond implied — large loss on short gamma
Black swan event causes gap that delta hedging cannot handle
VRP regime shifts — extended period of negative premium

strategies.name: Volatility Skew Mean Reversion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The implied volatility skew (difference between OTM put IV and OTM call IV) mean-reverts around its historical average. When skew is unusually steep (puts expensive relative to calls), selling the expensive puts and buying cheap calls (risk reversal) captures the normalization. Edge is that skew is driven by hedging demand from institutional equity holders, creating a persistent but mean-reverting premium in put volatility.
strategies.entry_logic: When |skew_zscore| > entry_zscore, trade the normalization. If skew is too steep (zscore > threshold): sell OTM puts, buy OTM calls (short skew). If too flat (zscore < -threshold): buy OTM puts, sell OTM calls (long skew). Delta-hedge the package to isolate the skew view. Prefer 30-45 DTE options for theta/gamma balance.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when skew_zscore returns to within exit_zscore_band. Stop loss if skew extends to stop_zscore. Roll to new options if approaching expiry with position still open.

target_dte.name: target_dte
target_dte.description: Target days to expiry for options

known_failure_modes: Skew blowout during crash — short puts face extreme mark-to-market loss
Skew regime shift (structural change in hedging demand)
Options liquidity dries up, making exit costly

strategies.name: IV Term Structure Calendar Trade
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: The implied volatility term structure (IV across expiries) oscillates between contango (longer-dated IV higher) and backwardation (shorter-dated IV higher). Extreme states mean-revert because the structural drivers (event risk, hedging demand) are transient. By selling the relatively expensive tenor and buying the relatively cheap tenor (calendar spread), the strategy captures the normalization. Edge is term structure mean-reversion, which is more reliable than directional vol bets.
strategies.entry_logic: When term_spread_zscore exceeds entry_zscore (term structure is extreme), trade the normalization. Steep contango: sell back-month, buy front-month (short calendar). Steep backwardation: buy back-month, sell front-month (long calendar). Both at ATM strikes. Verify no major event falls between the two expiries (which would justify the term structure shape).
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when term_spread_zscore normalizes (within exit_zscore). Roll positions if front month approaches expiry. Stop loss if term structure extends to stop_zscore.

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct per calendar trade

known_failure_modes: Event between tenors justifies the extreme term structure
Front month pin risk at expiry
Vol-of-vol spike makes both tenors move against the spread

strategies.name: Index vs Single-Stock Dispersion
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: Index options are structurally overpriced relative to single-stock options because institutional demand for portfolio hedging concentrates in index puts. This creates a "correlation risk premium" — the index IV implies higher correlation than typically realizes. By selling index options and buying a basket of single-stock options (dispersion trade), the strategy captures this premium. Edge is the structural demand imbalance for index-level hedging.
strategies.entry_logic: When implied_correlation exceeds realized_correlation by more than correlation_premium_threshold, sell index straddle and buy a basket of single-stock straddles weighted by index composition. Delta-hedge the package. Scale by magnitude of the correlation premium.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when correlation_risk_premium narrows below exit_premium. Stop loss if implied correlation drops below realized (premium inverts — rare but dangerous). Hold to expiry if premium remains positive. Close before any major scheduled event (earnings season).

correlation_premium_threshold.name: correlation_premium_threshold
correlation_premium_threshold.description: Min correlation premium (implied minus realized) to enter

basket_size.name: basket_size
basket_size.description: Number of single stocks in the basket

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct for dispersion trade

known_failure_modes: Correlation spike during systemic sell-off devastates the trade
Single-stock vol explodes on idiosyncratic events
Transaction costs on multi-leg trade erode the premium

strategies.name: VIX Futures Contango Roll
strategies.alpha_hypothesis: VIX futures are in contango approximately 80% of the time (front month cheaper than second month) because of the volatility risk premium — investors pay to be long vol as insurance. The contango creates a persistent negative roll yield for VIX long holders and a positive roll yield for short holders. By systematically shorting the second-month VIX future and buying the front month (or shorting VIX ETPs like VXX), the strategy harvests the roll-down. Edge is the structural insurance premium embedded in VIX futures.
strategies.entry_logic: When contango_pct exceeds min_contango_threshold AND vix_spot is below max_vix_entry (not in a vol spike), short the second month VIX future against long front month. Or simply short a VIX ETP that rolls. Scale size inversely with VIX level (lower VIX = more comfortable being short vol). Enter when term structure zscore confirms normal contango regime.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when contango inverts to backwardation (VIX spike). Exit when VIX spot exceeds emergency_vix_level. Roll positions monthly before front month expiry. Reduce size when VIX approaches the upper comfort zone.

max_vix_entry.name: max_vix_entry
max_vix_entry.description: Max VIX spot level to enter short

max_position_pct.name: max_position_pct
max_position_pct.description: Max portfolio pct for VIX short

known_failure_modes: Volmageddon-style event — VIX spikes 100%+ in a day, massive loss
Sustained backwardation during extended vol regime
VIX ETP structure changes or is delisted
